If every Signe, then containes in longitude thirty degrees, and every degree flxty
If Two Planets are equally distant sixty degrees from each other, then They are in a Sextile Aspect
If Two Planets are ninety degrees distant from each other, then That Aspect is a Quartile Aspect
If Planets are one hundred and twenty degrees distant, then They are in a Trine Aspect
If Two Planets are one hundred and eighty degrees distant, then That Aspect is an Opposition
If Two Planets are in one and the same degree and minute of any Sign, then They are in Conjunction
If Saturn is in the first degree of Aries, and the Moon or any other Planet is in the first degree of Gemini, then They are in a Sextile Aspect, which is indifferent good
If Saturn or any other Planet is in the first degree of Aries, and another Planet is in the first degree of Cancer, then They are in a Quartile Aspect, which is of enmity and not good
If Saturn is in the first degree of Aries and any Planet in the first degree of Sagittarius, then They behold each other with a Trine Aspect, and this doth denote Unity, Concord and Friendship
If Saturn is in the first degree of Aries, and any Planet in the first degree of Libra, then They are said to be in Opposition: A bad Aspect

If Saturn is in the first degree of Aries, and any Planet is in the same degree, then They are then said to be in Conjunction: And this Aspect is good or ill, according to the nature of the question demanded
If Moon in aspect with the Sun, then Change of the Moon
If Moon in quartile aspect after conjunction with the Sun, then First quarter of the Moon
If Moon in opposition with the Sun, then Full Moon
If Moon in quartile aspect after opposition with the Sun, then Last quarter of the Moon
If Venus in greatest elongation from the Sun, then Venus in greatest elongation
If Sun and Venus in Quincunx, then Sun and Venus are in a Quincunx aspect
If Moon and Venus in Semisextile, then Moon and Venus are in Semisextile aspect
If Saturn and Sun in Quartile, then Saturn and Sun are in a Quartile aspect
If Sun and Saturn in Quintile, then Sun and Saturn have a Quintile aspect
If Sign T is not upon any of the Cufpes of Houses, but is included betwixt one House and another, then the Signe T is intercepted
If Planet is in more Degrees then the Gufpe of the House, then Planet is placed within the House
If Planet's Degrees are less than those of the Cufp of the Houses, then Planet is placed without the House
If Planet is neerer in Degrees to the Cufpe of any House, having the same Signe, then Planet ought to be placed to the Cuipe of that Houle
If Tenth house has Signe T, then Cusp of the sourth has Signe and
If Signe of the tenth house has degree and minute, then lame degree and minute of the opposite Signe muft be placed on the Cufp of the sourth house
If Figure be ereanded from noon to Sun set, then the Sun will be in the ninth, eighth or seventh house
If Figure be set from Sun let till midnight, then he shall sind the Sun in the sixt, sift or sourth house
If Figure be let from midnight till Sun rise, then he shall sind the Sun in the third, second or first house
If Figure be set from Sun rise till noon, then then he shall sind the Sun in the twelfth, elenth or tenth house
If Figure be set six or seven hours after noon, then adde about 15 min. to their places at noon
If allowing sor every six hours, then i ^ min. motion
If Moon goeth 12, 13 or 14 degr. in one day, then constantly adde to her place at noon 3 degr. sor every six hours

If sind another Planet in aspect or conjunction with that Planet who is the principall Signisicator, then this said Planet shall have signisication more or less, and either assist or not In effecting the thing desired, and hath something to doe in the Judgment, and ought to be considered
If a friendly Planet in aspect or conjunction with that Planet who is the principall Signisicator, then he notes good
If an infortune in aspect or conjunction with that Planet who is the principall Signisicator, then the contrary, viz . either the destruction of the thing, or disturbance in it
If Aries or Taurus be in the first house, then you shall observe some
If T or ^3 in the first house at the time of a Q^iedion, or at the time of birth, then some blemish in the face, or in that member appropriate to the Signe that then is upon the cusp of the house
If T? in the Ascendant, then the mark, mole, or scarre is without sade in the head or face
If few degrees of the Signe ascend, then the mark is in the upper part of the head
If the middle of the Sign be on the cusp, then the mole, mark or scar is in the middle of the face, or neer it
If the later degrees ascend, then the face is blemished neer the chin, towards the neck
If a Planet be in this house that hath signification of white, then the complexion of the Party is more pale, white or wan
If his significator be ill the first house, and in a Signe corresponding, then the parties apparell is white or gray, or somewhat neer that colour
If Significators are found in this house, then denotes them to be of that colour or neer it
If Saturn is but moderately well fortified, in this house, and in any benevolent aspect of Venus, Jupiter, or Moon, then promiseth a good sober constitution of body, and usually long life
If Mercury is well dignified and posited in this house, then produced good Orators
If Venus be placed in this house, or be Lord hereof, then it’s an argument of an estate or fortune
If Jupiter and Moon are never well placed in this house, then either of them shewd.spersion of substance, according to the capacity and quality of him that is either borne or asks the questions
If Mercury in this house, unless joyned with Jupiter, then is not very unfortunate
If Jupiter be posited therein, especially in a moveable Signe, then it’s an argument of much travel!, flitting and trudging, or of being seldome quiet
If Sun be herein placed, then he is not ill but rather shewes the Father to be of a noble disposition
If Mars or Saturn therein, then I shew disobedient children and untoward
If Moon rejoyceth in this house, then None
If Saturn or Mercury unfortunate herein, then show ill in Marriage
If Jupiter be herein placed, then it naturally signifies a devout man in his Religion, or one modestly given
If Dragons tayle, or and or F have been infortunately placed in this House, then the Querent hath either, been little better then an Atheist or a desperate Sectarist
If the Sun rejoyceth to be in this House, then None
If Jupiter or the Sun doe much Fortunate this House when they are policed therein, then None
If Saturn or Moon usually deny honour, as to persons of quality, or but little esteeme in the world to a vulgar person, not much joy in his Profession, then None
If V or cf 5 are posited in the tenth house, then fortunate

If <0 is posited in the tenth house, then usually deny honour, as to persons of quality, or but little esteem in the world to a vulgar person, not much joy in his Profession, Trade or Mystery, if a Mechanic
If Saturn rejoices in the twelfth house, then Saturn is author of mischief
If Saturn is in any of these degrees wherein he hath a Terme, then he cannot be said to be peregrinee, or void of essentiall dignities
If Saturn is in any of those degrees alotted him for his Face or Decanate, then he cannot then be said to be peregrinee
If Saturn is Oriental of the Sun, then the stature is more short, but decent and well composed
If Saturn is Oriental of the Sun, then the man is more black and leane, and sewer Hairs
If Saturn want latitude, then the body is more leane
If Saturn have great latitude, then the body is more sat or fleshy
If Saturn latitude be Meridionall or South, then more fleshy, but quick in motion
If Saturn latitude be North, then hairy and much flesh
If Saturn is in his first station, then a little sat
If Saturn is in his second station, then sat, ill savoured Bodies, and weak
If Ruptures if in Scorpio or L?c, in any ill aspect with Venus, then Ruptures

If the Moon applying by a Trine or Sextile to Saturn in an Angle, either in the Ascendant, or tenth, or eleventh house, then the Ancients did observe to turne their faces towards the East in his hour,and he,if possible,in an Angle,either in the Ascendant, or tenth, or eleventh house
If Saturn applying by aspect or planet applying to Saturn within nine degrees, then Saturn's influence begins to work
If Saturn separating by aspect nine degrees, then Saturn's influence ceases
If Saturn is essentially and accidentally strong when a new building, town, city, family, or principality is begun, then The family, principality, etc., may continue 465 years in honor
If Saturn is well dignified and Lord of the Geniture in a nativity, then The native may live 57 years
If Saturn is meanly dignified in a nativity, then The native may live 43 years
If Saturn is Lord of the Nativity and weak, then The child may live 30 years, hardly any more

If Jupiter is well dignified in the Ascendant in a nativity, then The person is magnanimous, faithful, bashful, aspiring, a lover of fair dealing, desiring to benefit all men, doing glorious things, honorable and religious, of sweet and affable conversation, wonderfully indulgent to his wife and children, reverencing aged men, a great reliever of the poor, full of charity and godliness, liberal, hating all sordid actions, just, wise, prudent, thankful, virtuous

If Jupiter is unfortunate, then The person wastes his patrimony, suffers everyone to cozen him, is hypocritically religious, tenacious, and stiff in maintaining false tenets in religion, he is ignorant, careless, nothing delightful in the love of his friends; of a grosse, dull capacity, schismatical, abating himself in all companies, crooching and hooping where no necessity is
If Jupiter is very powerful in essential or accidental dignities and the Moon is in good aspect with Jupiter and in some of his dignities, then Good for gathering herbs appropriated to Jupiter
If Mars is dignified, then The person will have good manners
If Mars is oriental, then signifies valiant men, some white or mixed with their redness, a decent tallness of body, hairy of body
If Mars is occidental, then Very ruddy complexion’d, but mean in stature, little head, a smooth body, and not hairy; yellow hair, stiff, the natural humours generally more dry
If Sun is well dignified, then Equivalent to a Fortune

If Sun presents a man, then good, large and strong corporature, a yellow, saffron complexion, a round, large forehead, goggle eyes or large, sharp and piercing, a body strong and well composed, not so beautiful as lovely, full of heat, their hair yellowish, and therefore quickly bald, much hair on their beard, and usually an high ruddy complexion, their bodies fleshy, in conditions they are very bountiful, honest, sincere, well-minded, of great and large heart, high-minded, of healthful constitution, very humane, yet sufficiently spirited, not loquacious
If Sun is Oriental in the Figure, then Oriental
If Sun is in the Oriental quarter of the Figure, then Oriental
If Sun is Occidental, then Occidental

If Sun signifies, then Kings, Princes, Emperors, Dukes, Marquesses, Earls, Barons, Lieutenants, Deputy-Lieutenants of Counties, Magistrates, Gentlemen in general, Courtiers, desirers of Honour and preferment, Justices of Peace, Majors, High-Sheriffs, High-Constables, great Huntsmen, Lieutenants, Deputy-Lieutenants, Stewards of Noble-mens houses, the principal Magistrate of any City, Towne, Castle or Country Village, yea, though a petty Constable, where no better, or greater Officer is; Goldsmiths, Brasiers, Pewterers, Coppersmiths, Minters of Money

If Sun signifies, then Pimples in the Face, Palpitation or Trembling, or any Diseases of the Braine or Heart, Timpanies Infirmities of the Eyes, Cramps, Hidden swoonings, Diseases of the Mouth, and stinking Breaths, Catars, rotten Feavers
If Sun governs in man, then the Heart, the Braine and right Eye, and vital Spirit
If Sun governs in women, then the left Eye
If Sun rules colours, then Yellow, the colour of Gold, the Scarlet or the deer Red, some say Purple
If Sun likes savours, then a mixture of Sour and Sweet together, or the Aromatical savour, being a little Bitter and Stiptical but withal Consortative and a little sharp

If Plants are subject to the Sun, then smell pleasantly, are of good savour, their Flowers are yellow or reddish, are in growth of Majestical forme, they love open and Sunshine places, their principal Vertue is to strengthen the Heart, and comfort the Vitals, to deer the Eye sight, resist Poyson, or to dissolve any Witchery, or Malignant Planetary Influences
If Sun has dominion of, then fire and cleere shining flames
If Sun rules over metals, then Gold
If Sun produces weather in the Spring, then gentle moysting Showers
If Sun produces weather in the Summer, then heat in extremity if with ?
If Sun produces weather in Autumn, then mists
If Sun produces weather in Winter, then small Raine
If Sun loves, then the East part of the World, and that winde which proceeds from that quarter
If Venus signifies a quiet man, then not given to Law, Quarrel or Wrangling, not Vicious, Pleasant, Neat and Spruce, loving Mirth well placed

If Venus signifies a man, then Mirth in his words and actions, cleanly in Apparel, rather Drinking much then Gluttonous, prone to Venery, oft entangled in Love-matters. Zealous in their affections, Musicall, delighting in Baths, and all honest merry Meetings, or Maskes and Stage-playes, easie of Beliese, and not given to Labour, or take any Pains, a Companyskeeper, Cheerful, nothing Mistrustful, a right vertuous Man or Woman, oft had in some Jealousie, yet no cause for it

If Venus signifies a man, then Riotous, Expensive, wholly given to Looseness and Lewd companies of Women, nothing regarding his Reputation, coveting unlawful Beds, Inceftuous, an Adulterer Fanatical, a meer Skip-jack, of no Faith, no Repute, no Credit; spending his Meanes in Ale houses, Taverns, and amongst Scandalous, Loose people; a meer Lazy companion, nothing careful of the things of this Life, or any thing Religious; a meer Atheist and Natural man

If Venus signifies a man, then of faire, but not tall Stature, his Complexion being white, tending to a little darknesse, which makes him more Lovely; very fair Lovely Eyes, and a little black; a round Face, and not large, faire Haire, smooth, and plenty, of it, and it usually of a light browne colour, a lovely Mouth and cherry Lips, the Face pretty fleshy, a rolling wandring Eye, a Body very delightful, lovely and exceeding well shaped, one desirous of Trimming and making himself neat and compleat both in Cloaths and Body, a love dimple in his Cheeks, a stedfast Eye, and full of amarous enticements
If Venus is Oriental, then the Body inclines to talnesse, or a kind of upright straightnesse in Person, not corpulent or very tail, but neatly composed
If Venus is Oriental, then a pretty, compleat, handsome Man or Woman
If Venus is Occidental, then the Man is of more short stature, yet very decent and comely in Shape and Forme, well liked of all

If Venus signifies, then Musitions, Gamefters, Silk-men, Mercery Linnen-Drapers, Painters, Jewellers, Players, Lapidaries, Embroiderers, Women-tailors, Wives, Mothers, Virgins, Choristers, Fidlers, Tipers, when joyned with the Ballad-singers, Perfumers, Semsters, Picture-drawers, Gravers, Upholdders, Limners, Glovers, all such as sell those Commodities which adorne Women, either in Body (as Cloaths) or in Face, (as Complexion-waters)

If Diseases are signified by Venus, then principally in the Matrix and members of Generation, in the reines, belly, backe, navill and those parts, the Gonorrhea or running of the Peines, French or Spanish Pox, any disease arising by inordinate lust. Priapism, impotency in generation, Hernias, the Diabetes or pining disease
If Venus signifies colours, then White, or milky Skie colour mixed with browne, or a little Greene
If Venus delights in savours, then that which is pleasant and tooth some, usually in moyst and sweet, or what is very delectable
If Venus delights in smells, then what is odious and Aromaticall, and incites to wantonnesse
If Herbs which Venus governs, then have a sweet savour, a pleasant smell, a white flower, of a gentle humour, whose leaves are smooth and not jagged
If Venus governs the South-winde, then hot and moist
If Venus is in the temperance of the Ayre, then she ruleth the MeesuQ
If Venus, then foretells Summer, Serenity or cleer weather
If Venus is in Winter, then raine or snow
If Mercury is well dignified, then represents a man of a subtill and politick braine, intellect, and cogitation
If Mercury is well dignified, then an excellent disputant or Logician, arguing with learning and discretion, and using much eloquence in his speech
If Mercury is well dignified, then a searcher into all kinds of Mysteries, Learning, (harpe and witty, learning almoit any thing without a Teacher

If Mercury is well dignified, then ambitious of being exquchte m every Science, desirous naturally of travell and seeing soraign parts a man of an unwearied sancie, curious in the learch of any occult knowledge
If Mercury is well dignified, then able by his owne Genius to produce wonders ; given to Divination and the more secret knowledge
If Mercury is well dignified and turns Merchant, then no man exceeds him in way of Trade or invention of new wayes whereby to obtain wealth

If Mercury is ill-dignified, then a troublesome wit, a kinde of Herenetkk man,his tongue and Pen againft every man , wholly bent to soole his estate and tithe in pitting and trying nice condusions to no purpose
If Mercury is ill-dignified, then a great lyar, boaster, pratler, busibody, salfe, a tale-carrier, giv- to wicked Arts, as Necromancy, and such hke ungodly knowledges
If Mercury is ill-dignified, then easie of beleese, an afle or very ideot, conftant In no place or opinion, cheating and theeving every where
If Mercury is ill-dignified, then a newes-monger, pretending all manner of knowledge,but guil- ty of no true or iolid learning 5 a trifler y a meere frantick sel- low
If Mercury is ill-dignified and proves a Divine, then a meer verbal! sellow, frothy, of no /udgement,ea(ily perverted,conftant in nothing but idle words and bragging
If Mercury is Oriental, then his complexion is honey colour, or like one well Sun-burnt
If Mercury is Oriental, then intheftature of his body not very high, but well joynted, fmall eyes, not much haire , in very truth, according to the height of body, very well composed
If Mercury is Oriental, then dill adeseandin in the complexion, zdand.fwarty brown, and in the tongue, viz/ all sor his owne ends
If Mercury is Occidental, then a tawny visage, lanke body, fmall (len- der limbsjhollow eyes, and fparkling and red or siery$tlie whol frame of body inclining to drinefle
If Mercury applies to Saturn, then heavy
If Mercury applies to Jupiter, then more temperate
If Mercury applies to Mars, then more rash
If Mercury applies to the Sun, then more genteel
If Mercury applies to Venus, then more jelling
If Mercury applies to the Moon, then more shifter
If Mercury stirs up that Wind which the Planet signisies to which he applyes, then sometimes Raine, at other times Haile, Lightning, Thunder and Tempelb,in ho^rCotintdes Earthquakes
If Moon is slow in motion (less than 13 degrees 10 minutes in 24 hours), then Equivalent to a Retrograde Planet
If Head of the Dragon in conjunction with good planets, then Good
If Head of the Dragon in opposition with evil planets, then Evil
If Tail of the Dragon joined with good planets, then Evil
If Tail of the Dragon in conjunction with malignant planets, then Good
If X (Jupiter) in moist signs, then person comely and somewhat fat
If X (Jupiter) in airy signs, then person big and strong
If X (Jupiter) in earthly signs, then man usually well descended
If Mars in fiery or airy signs with fixed stars of his own nature, then shows a deep sandy red color (hair)
If Mars in watery signs with fixed stars of his own nature, then flaxenish or whitish bright hair
If Mars in earthly signs, then hair is like a sad brown, or of a sad chestnut color
If Mars in the 9th house, then wenches
If Mars in the 5th house, then steals
If Mars in his own house, then quarrels
If Mars in the 4th house, then dogged
If Mars in the Sun's house, then lordly
If Mars in the Moon's house, then drunkard
If Moon in fiery signs, then Man or Woman speaks hastily
If Moon in watery signs, then he or she has some freckles in his or her face, or is blub cheeked
If Lord of the Ascendant in a moveable sign and the sign ascending is also one, then person to be unstable, and of no resolution, easily mutable, perverted, a wavering inconstant man

If Ascendant is fixed, and the Lord of that Signe also in a fixed Signe, then party to be of firme resolution, no changing ; or as we say, one that will stand to maintaine what he hath said or done, be it good or evil
If Signe attending be common, and Lord of that Signe also in a Common Signe, then man or woman to be neither very wilfull or easily variable but betweene both
If Venus in mute signs in hard aspect to Mercury, then of slow voice
If Significator or Lord of the Ascendant in Aries, then The person is rash, hardy, and lascivious.
If Significator or Lord of the Ascendant in Taurus, then The person is steadfast and resolved, and somewhat of a muddy condition, vitiated, with some private imperfection.
If Moon and principal significators in prolific signs and strong, then The person will have children.
If Ascendant or fifth house in barren signs, then Represents few or no children.
If Gemini, Libra, or Aquarius ascend, then The man is of civil carriage, very affable and easy to be spoken with.
If Lord of the Ascendant in humane signs, then The man is of civil carriage, very affable and easy to be spoken with.

If Sun in Leo, then sicknesses in the ribs and sides, as Pleurisies, Convulsions, Diseases in the backe, trembling or passion of the heart, violent burning- seavers, all weaknesse or diseases in the heart, sore eyes, Plague the Pestilence, the yellow-Jaundies

If Moon in Cancer, then Imperfeandions all over, or in the ^leit. Stomas and Pap', weakDigeftion, cold Stomack, Peisick, sait Flegms, roten Coughs, dropficall Humours, Impoftumations in the Stomack, Cancers which ever are sin the Breft

If Mars in Scorpio, then Gravel!, the Stone in the Secret parts, Bladder, Ruptures, Fiilulaes, or the Pyles in ^^Gonorrhea’s, Priapifmes,all affiianding the Privy parts either in man or woman $ desers in the Matrix

If Jupiter in Sagittarius, then ruleth the Thighes and Buttocks in the parts of mans body, and all FiFulaes or Hurts salling in those members,and generally denoted Flood heated, Feayers Peftilentiall, sals from Horses, or hurts from them or sour-sooted Beafts j alfb prejudice by Fire, Heat and intemperateneffe in Sports
If Saturn in Capricorn, then government of the Knees, and all Diseases incident to those p] zees, either by Straines or Fraandures 3 it notes Leprosie, the Itch, the Scab
If Mercury in Virgo and Moon in Cancer, then a rare understanding, but somewhat unstable

If Sign ascending is humane and aerial, and the lord of that sign or the Moon is in any sign of the same triplicity or nature, then The body is handsome, and the conditions of the party are sociable, or he is very courteous
If Mars is either on the cusp of the Ascendant, or descending in the sixth house, then The disease has something in its nature of the nature of Aries
If Significator of a lost thing is in Aries, then Search for the lost thing in places that Aries directs to
If Significator is fortunate in Aries, or Jupiter or Venus are therein, then One may safely travel or sojourn in such cities or countries as the sign of Aries represents
If A planet is in its house, then The planet is essentially strong

If A significator is in its own house, then It represents a man in such a condition, as that he is lord of his own house, estate and fortune; or a man wanting very little of the goods of this world, or it tells you the man is in a very happy state or condition
If Significator is retrograde, combust, or afflicted by any other malevolent planet or aspect, then The significator being in its own house will not be true
If A planet is in that sign wherein it is exalted, then Allow it four essential dignities
If Significator is in its exaltation, and noways impeded, but angular, then It presents a person of haughty condition, arrogant, assuming more unto him than his due
If A planet is in any of those signs which are allotted to it for its triplicity, then Allow it three dignities

If A planet is in its triplicity, then It shows a man modestly indued with the goods and fortune of this world, one prettily descended, and the condition of his life at present time of the question, to be good
If A planet is in those degrees we assign for its terms, then Allow it two dignities

If A planet is fortified, only as being in its own terms, then It rather shows a man more of the corporature and temper of the planet, than any extraordinary abundance in fortune, or of eminency in the commonwealth
If A planet is in its decanate, or face, then It is then allowed one essential dignity; for being in his own decanate or face, cannot then be called peregrine

If A planet having little or no dignity, but by being in its senate or face, then It is almost like a man ready to be turned out of doors, having much ado to maintain himself in credit and reputation: and in genealogies it represents a family at the last gasp, even as good as quite decayed, hardly able to support itself
If A planet having little or no dignity, but being in its Senate or Face, then is almost like a man ready to be turned out of doors, having much ado to maintain himself in credit and reputation
If A planet having little or no dignity, but being in its Senate or Face, then represents a Family at the last gasp, even as good as quite decayed, hardly able to support itself
If Planets Direct, swift in Motion, Angular, in aspect with Jupiter or Venus, or in conjunction with certain notable fixed Stars, then Planets may be strong
If Quadrate aspect, then a signe of imperfect enmity
If Opposition aspect, then an argument of perfect hatred
If Sextile and trine aspects, then arguments of Love, Unity and Friendship
If The trine aspect, then more forcibly signifies peace
If Two significators are in trine or sextile, then no doubt but peace may be assuredly concluded
If Conjunctions, then good or bad, as the Planets in conjunction are friends or enemies to one another
If A Partill aspect signifies good, then argument for performance of anything, or that the matter is neere hand concluded
If A Partill aspect signifies evil, then signe of present evill when mischiefe is threatened
If Two Planets are retrograde, then an ill Application and an argument either suddenly perfecting, or breaking off the businesse, according as the two Planets have signification
If A Planet is direct, and in fewer degrees, and a retrograde Planet being in more degrees of the Signe, then an ill application, and in the Ayre shewes great change; in a Question sudden alteration
If Superior Planets do not apply to the inferior (unless they be Retrograde), then ever the lighter to the more ponderous
If Planet in Aries casts a square or trine to any planet in Leo or Sagittarius in like degrees, then Sinister square or trine
If Saturn in Aries casts a square or trine to any planet in Capricorn or Taurus, then Dexter aspect
If A planet in Aries and another in Leo in like degrees, then Aries beholds Leo with a trine dexter aspect
If A planet in Aries and another in Capricorn, then Aries beholds Capricorn with a square dexter aspect
If A planet in Aries beholding another in Libra, then Calls his opposition aspect unto him
If Aries beholds Gemini with a Sinister sextile, then Aries beholds Gemini with a Sinister sextile
If Aries beholds Cancer with a Sinister square, then Aries beholds Cancer with a Sinister square
If Aries beholds Virgo with a Sinister trine, then Aries beholds Virgo with a Sinister trine
If Dexter aspects, then Contrary to the succession of signs
If Sinister aspects, then In order as they follow one another
If A planet in one sign cannot have any aspect to another in the sign underneath, then Signs not beholding one another
If Two planets are departed but six minutes distance from each other, then Separation

If Two planets, significators in marriage, are lately separated but a few minutes, then There had been but few days before great probability of effecting the marriage, but now it hung in suspense, and there seemed some dislike or rupture in it
If Significators separate more, then The matter and affection of the parties more alienate and vary
If Significators in moveable signs, angular and swift in motion, then Hasten the times

If Two planets that signify the effecting or bringing to conclusion anything demanded are applying to an aspect and before they can come to a true aspect, another planet interposes either his body or aspect, then The matter propounded is hindered and retarded
If Combustion of any planet, then The greatest misfortune that can be

If Two planets are going to conjunction and another planet being more swift, doth quickly overtake and pass by the dexter of one planet and comes before the other planet can come to conjunction to a sextile dexter of the first planet, then Prohibition by aspect

If A light planet separates from a more weighty one, and presently joins to another more heavy, then Translation of light and nature

If Two planets that are significators in any question or matter, are in each other's dignity by houses, then Reception of these two planets by houses, and certainly this is the strongest and best of all receptions
If Two planets that are significators in any question or matter, are in each other's dignity by triplicity, then Reception of these two planets by triplicity, if the question or nativity be by day
If Two planets that are significators in any question or matter, are in each other's dignity by term, then Reception of these two planets by term

If The effecting of a matter is denied by the aspects, or when the significators have no aspect to each other, or when it seems very doubtful what it's promised by square or conjunction of the significators yet mutual reception happen between the principal significators, then The thing is brought to pass, and that without any great trouble, and suddenly to the content of both parties

If A planet is separated from a planet, nor doth forthwith, during his being in that sign, apply to any other, then Planet is void of course

If Part of Fortune Combust, then Part of Fortune hath debilities

If Ascendant not afflicted, Lord of the Ascendant Essentially fortified, Moon in trine with both the Fortunes, Signs of long ascension, Jupiter and Venus in the South Angle, in Sagittarius, a South-east Signe; Sun in Taurus, an Easterly Signe; Moon in Aquarius, a Southerne or Southweft Signe, then Best to travell Southward, or a little East
If Jupiter and Venus in the Mid-heaven, and Moon and Sun in the ninth, then His younger yeers are most full of Pleasure
If Sun Lord of the Ascendant , lately separated from good and ill aspects; Moon also separated as well from conjunction of Saturn as trine of Jupiter, then Shew both good and ill had happened of late
If Evil aspects being more in number then the good, and signified by superiour Planets, then Augment the Evill and lessen the Good
If Moon applying to trine of Venus, the Sun Lord of the Ascendant in Exaltation, then Promise Preserment
If Moon weak in the second, and after her trine with Venus going to conjunction of Mars, then Shewes, after a little time of Joy, great danger
If The small distance of degrees between the trine of Moon to Jupiter, and Jupiter Angular, then Denote a present happinesse or fortune neer to the Querent
If Her greater distance from conjunction of Saturn, then Shews his miseries to succeed some yeers after his times of Honour are expired
If Lord of the seventh house be in any of the four Angles, then The party is at home with whom you would speak with
If Lord of the seventh, or Lord of that house from whom Judgment is required, be in any Succedant house, viz, the eleventh, second, sixth or eighth, then He is not far from home
If His Significator be in a Cadent house, then He is far from home

If Lord of the Ascendant applying to the Lord of the seventh house by any perfect aspect, the same day that you intend to goe visit him, then You may be assured either to meet him going to his house, or heare of him by the way where he is, sor he cannot be sarre absent

If Any Planet, or the Moon, separate from the Lord of the seventh house, and transfeire his light unto the, then You may be assured either to meet him going to his house, or heare of him by the way where he is, sor he cannot be sarre absent
If Significator in a cadent house, then far from home

If Lord of the Ascendant applying to the Lord of the seventh house by a perfect aspect, then assured to either meet him going to his house, or hear of him by the way where he is, for he cannot be farre absent

If Planet or Moon separates from the Lord of the seventh house and transfers light to the Lord of the Ascendant, then know where and in what place the Party is, by such a one as is signified by that Planet who transferres his light

If Lord of the Sign where the Sun is, Lord of the Sign where the Moon is, and Lord of the Ascendant are powerful in the Ascendant and in trine or sextile to Jupiter or Venus, then no evil chance upon the preceding Accident, Rumour or Report
If Planet weak in the scheme, combust, or in quartile or opposition to Saturn or Mars, then some misfortune will follow after that accident
If Lord of the third house afflicts the significator, then evil will chance from some Neighbour or Kinsman
If Lord of the second house impedites the significator, then loss in substance
If Lord of the fourth house impedites the significator, then expect discontent with one of your Parents, or about Land or Houses
If Lord of the fifth house impedites the significator, then some difference or discord in an Alehouse or Taverne, or in Company keeping, or by meanes of some Child
If Sign ascending, then Querent has a mole, scar or mark on the part of the body represented by that Sign
If Lord of the Ascendant in a Sign, then Querent has a mole, scar or mark on the part of the body represented by that Sign
If Sign descending on the cusp of the sixth house, then Querent has a mole, scar or mark on the part of the body represented by that Sign
If Lord of the sixth house in a Sign, then Querent has a mole, scar or mark on the part of the body represented by that Sign
If Moon in a Sign, then Querent has a mole, scar or mark on the part of the body represented by that Sign
If Saturn signifies the mark, then darkish, obscure, black mole
If Mars signifies the mark, then usually some Scarre or Cut
If Planet in a fiery Sign signifies the mark, then red mole
If Sign or Planet signifying the Mole, Mark or Scarre, be much afflicted, then Mark or Scarre is the greater and more eminent
If Sign is Masculine and Planet is Masculine, then Mole or Scar is on the right side of the body
If Sign is Feminine and Lord is in a Feminine Sign, then Mole or Scar is on the left side of the body
If Significator of the Scarre or Mole is above the earth, then Mark is on the forepart of the body, or visible to the eye, or on the out side of the member
If Significator of the Scarre or Mole is under the earth, then Mole or Scarre is on the back part of die body, not visible, but on the in side of the member
If Few degrees of a Sign ascend, or if the Lord of the Sign be in few degrees, then Mole, Mark or Scarre is in the upper part of the member
If Middle of the Sign ascend, or the Lord thereof in the middle, or neer the middle of the Sign, then Mole or Mirk is so in the member, viz . in the middle
If Latter degrees ascend, or the or Lord of the first or sixt house be neer the last degrees of the Sign, then Mole,Mark or Scir is rieere the lo wer part of the member
If Sign of the seventh house, then show the Womans Marks
If Sign wherein the Lord of the seventh is, then show the Womans Marks
If Sign upon the Cusp of the twelfth, then show two more Moles or Marks of the Woman
If Sign wherein the Lord of the twelfth is in, then show two more Moles or Marks of the Woman
If Infortune in the Ascendant, then blemishes the Face with some Mole or Scarre according to his nature
If Moon in quartile or opposition to the Sun, then querent hath some blemish or the like near one of his Eyes
If Moon or Sun in quartile or opposition to the Sun or Moon in Angles, and either of them have any ill aspect to Mars, then querent hath some blemish or the like near one of his Eyes
If Question demanded of one absent in a general way, and the querent has no relation to the party, then the first House, the Lord of that House and the Moon shall signifie the absent party
If Lord of the eight House or Planet posited either in the House or within five degrees of the Cusp of the 8th House, then show his death or its quality
If Lord of the Ascendant, the Moon and Lord of the eight House or Planet in the eight house be corporally joyned together, then the party is deceased,or sick,and very neer death
If Moon, Lord of the Ascendant and Lord of the eight are in opposition either in the eight and second, or twelfth and sixt, then the party is deceased,or sick,and very neer death
If translation of the light of the Lord of the Ascendant unto the Lord of the eight, especially in degrees deepe, lame or desicient, then the party is deceased,or sick,and very neer death
If translation or carrying the vertue or influence of the Lord of the eight unto the Lord of the Ascendant, then the party is deceased,or sick,and very neer death
If Lord of the eight be posited in the Ascendant, then the party is deceased,or sick,and very neer death
If Lord of the Ascendant and the Moon be placed in the fourth House, then the party absent is dead
If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the sixt, then the absent hath been lately sicke
If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the eight, then he hath been in danger of death, but is not dead
If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the twelfth, then he hath been lately much troubled in minde, in seare ol imprisonment, arrefts,etc
If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the second, then he hath been hard put to it for money, or in distresie for want
If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of theseventh, then in some quarrell or contention

If Lord of the Ascendant be separated from a bad Aspect of the Lord of the ninth or third, then he hath been crossed in his journey (if he was at Sea by contrary windes,or Pyrats)rf at Land by Theeves, bad Wayes, andc
If Lord of the Ascendant be in the ninth, tenth, or eleventh, then the absent was alive
If Lord of the eleventh and Lord of the Ascendant come to a trine or sextile Aspect, then about that time, if not that day, newes will be had of him
If Moon apply to a trine or sextile of the Lord of the Ascendant, then newes will be had of him
If Lord of the eleventh and Lord of the Ascendant come to a square or trine aspect, then news will be had of him
If Moon applies to a square or trine of the Lord of the Ascendant, then news will be had of him
If Lord of the 5th house is in the Ascendant, then the party sought after was at home at his mother's house at time of the question
If Moon applies to a dexter trine of Saturn, Lord of the 4th house, then the youth was at his mother's, and that she should find him there at her coming home
If Lord of the 5th is in the 10th house, then he would have been adjudged him at his Master's house
If Moon separated from Saturn, Signisicator of the Youth, and presently applied to a good or indifferent aspect of the Sun in an Angle, then he would have been adjudged him at his Master's house
If Significators come to a square or trine aspect, then you shall have a letter, or news of the party quesited after
If Significators come to a square or trine aspect and the Party enquiring, and Person inquired after be not farre asunder, then they meet that very day
If Lord of the 3rd house is in an Angle, then you might safely have adjudged, she should have found any one of these at home
If Lord of the 7th is not in an Angle, then he is not at home
If Lord of the 7th is in a Succedant house, then he is not far from home

If Sun is most powerful in the Ascendant, as being her house, and she having a Terme therein, and calling her sinister unto the cusp of the house, as also, being in trine with Jupiter, and he in the Ascendant, then it could not have redounded to the Querent's disadvantage, but rather good
If Sun is nearer to the cusp of the 2nd, then the Querent would have received some losse shortly
If Lord of the eleventh applies to a square of Jupiter, then the Querent to have news of the absent about ten weeks from the time of the Question
If Lord of the eleventh applies to a square of Jupiter and the absent be known to be at a neer distance, then in ten dayes they should heare of him because the Signes are moveable
If malevolent Planet by position be placed in the Ascendant, He having dignities in the eighth, then the Ship is lost, and the men drowned
If the Lord of the Ascendant in the eighth, in any ill configuration with the Lord of the eighth twelfth, sourth or sixth, then the Ship is lost, and the men drowned
If Moon combusi, then the Ship is lost, and the men drowned
If Moon under the earth, then the Ship is lost, and the men drowned
If Ascendant and Moon be infortunate and the Lord of the Ascendant sortunate, then the Ship is like to be drowned, but the men will be saved
If fortunate Planets are posited in the Angles, then the Ship will goe sase to the place intended, with all the Goods and Loading in her
If fortunate Planets are salling into Angles, then the Ship will goe sase to the place intended, with all the Goods and Loading in her
If Infortunes remote from Angles, Cadent, Combuft or under the Sun Beames, then the Ship will goe sase to the place intended, with all the Goods and Loading in her
If Infortunes in Angles, then there will chance some hinder ance unto the Ship, and it (hall be in that part which the Sigfce signisies where die unfortunate Planet is
If Infortunes in succeeding Houses, then there will chance some hinder ance unto the Ship, and it (hall be in that part which the Sigfce signisies where die unfortunate Planet is
If Saturn is in any of his Effemiall Dignities,or behold a place where he hath any Dignity or be in an earthly Signe, then very great danger and damage to the Ship

If Fortunes caft their benevolent ray es or afpeft to the places where Mars or Saturn are, and the Lords of the sour Angles of the Figure,and efpecially,or more properly, the Ascendant, and Lord of that house or Signe where the Moon is in be free, then the Ship (hall labour hard, and differ much damage, yet notwithftanding the greater part both of Goods and Men (hall be predrved
If Mars doe afflict the Lords of the Angles, and Difpositor of the Moon, then the Mariners will be in great seare of their Enemies,or of Pyrates or Sea-robbers, (hall even tremble sor seare of them

If unfortunate Signes (viz. those which are affiianded by die presence of Saturn, Mars or Moon) be those which signisie the bottome or that part of the Ship which is under Water, then argument of the breaking and drowning thereof, or receiving some dangerous Leak
If Signe wherein Saturn of the unfortunate Planet be the Stone of the sourth house, then siring of the Ship in the bottome
If Mars inftead of Moon doe denunciate dammage, and be placed in the Mid-heaven, then the Ship shall receive prejudice by contrary Winds, and by leaks in the Ship, by rending or uling of bad Sailes
If Infortune be in the Ascendant, then some losie will be in the sore-part of the Ship
If Lord of the Ascendant be Retrograde, then the Ship will proceed sorward a while, but either returne or put into sorae Harbour within a little time after her letting sorth

If Lord of the Ascendant be in a moveable Signe and Retrograde, and the Lord of the sourth also, viz. Retrograde, then the Ship will returne againe croffed by contrary Winds, to the very Port from which Ihe sirft set out

If Lord of the eighth shall infortunate the Lord of the Ascendant, especially if the Lord of the Ascendant be in the eighth, then there will come hurt to the Ship according to the nature of the Planet afflicting

If Planet that is Lord of the eighth house doe impedite the Lord of the house of the Ascendant, the Lord of the Ascendant and the Moon, then imports the death of the Mailer or Governour of the Ship, and of his Mate and principall Officers of the Ship
If Part of Fortune and the Lord of the second house be both unfortunate, then pronounced lofle in sale of thole Goods in the Ship,or ill venting of them, or that they will not come to a good Market
If Jupiter and Venus be in the second house, or Lords thereof, or Difpositors of the Signe the Moon is in, then there will good prosit ariie from the Voyage of that; Ship, and sale of Goods therein
If Lord of the Ascendant and Lord of the house ofthe Moon be slow of courfe, and those Planets that difpose of them, then probable the Ship will be slow in her motion, and make a long Voyage of it
If aforesaid Signisicdtors be quick in motion, then the Ship shall make good Ipeed to the Port intended, and will returne home againe in Ihorter time then is expeanded

If square or opposition afpeft betwixt the Lord of the Ascendant and Lord of that Signe who dilposeth the Moon, and this alpeand be without reception, then there will be much diseord amongft the Saylors9 and much controverfie betwixt the Merchant and them

If Lord ofthe second be removed from his second, (that Is, if be the Cusp of the lecond, and Venus surther removed then Saturn ) or if the Lord of the lecond be removed from the lecond houle wherein the Moon is in, then
If Lord of the Ascendant is strongest, then Sea-men shall prevail
If Lord of the house where the Moon is, is best fortified, then Merchant shall prevail
If Lord of the second house is removed from his second house (further than the Moon), then Ship-men will have scarcity of provision of vianduals and food
If Lord of the second house is removed from the second house wherein the Moon is, then Ship-men will have scarcity of provision of vianduals and food
If Disposer of the Moon is not with it, then Ship-men will have scarcity of provision of vianduals and food
If Planets or Moon are in Watery Signs, then Want of fresh water will most annoy the sailors
If Significators are in Earthly or Airy Signs, then Want of Food, Vianduals and Fire will oppresse them

If Ascendant afflicted by the square of Saturn and presence of fixed stars of his like nature, then Ship was much of the nature of Saturn, viz., a sluggish, heavy one, and of no good speed, or very sound

If Moon is Lady of the Ascendant, is posited in the eleventh house, and in her Exaltation, is no manner of way impedited, but by a benevolent aspect applying to a trine of Saturn and Sun, and is by bodily presence so neere unto Jupiter, and all the Significators above the Earth, then Ship was not cast away, but was living, and that the Sayldrs and Officers of the Ship were lively and in good condition
If No Infortunes in Angles, then Ship was not cast away, but was living, and that the Sayldrs and Officers of the Ship were lively and in good condition
If Moon was fixed, and locally in the eleventh house, then Ship was in some Harbour

If Moon applies to a trine of Saturn and Sun, and they in an Angle, and was her selfe as well as they, very swift in motion, and did want but a sew minutes of their perfect trine, then There would be newes or Letters, or a certaine discovery of the Ship in a very short time
If Moon disposed by Jupiter and Saturn to whom the Moon applied to be in reception with Venus, then Merchant should encrease his Stock, and not loss by that adventure
If Saturn has his Antifcion in the ninth of Gemini, the very Cusp of the second house and and his Antifcion falls upon the very degree ascending, then Good testimonies of safety
If Moon applies to a good aspect of a Retrograde Planet, then It brings the matter to an end one way or other speedily, and when least suspended
If Moon apply to the Fortunes, or by good aspect to any Planet or Planets in Angles, then There is reason we hope well
If Ascendant free from presence of Infortunes, then Good sign
If Lord of the Ascendant above the Earth, and the Moon and their Dispositors, good signes, then Good sign
If Lord of the Ascendant in tenth, eleventh or ninth houses, then Good
If Lord of the Ascendant in trine or with Jupiter or Lord of the eleventh, then Good

If Moon lately separated from a square of Saturn, Lord of the eighth and ninth, then at time of the Question void of course; but afterwards first applied to a trine of Saturn; then to sextile of Jupiter, Lord of the twelfth and fourth, then Ship had lately been in danger (of Death) and shipwrack
If Moon was void of course, then No newes been heard of her

If Moon was left in square of Saturn, in fixed Signes, tortuous or odiously and malitiously aspecting each other, and falling into Cadent houses, and then did not presently apply to the good aspect of any benevolent Planet, but was voyd of course; and then againe continued her application out of the fourth to Saturn, who is still Lord of the eighth, although it was by good aspect, then Ship had lately been in danger (of Death) and shipwrack

If Moon after Reparation from Saturn, applyed to sextile Jupiter, and that Moon her Dispositor was if his Detriment, and entring Combustion, and Venus Dispositor of Subterranean and in trine with Mars, and teritories of an Infortune, then Ship had lately been in danger (of Death) and shipwrack
If Saturn in his Fall, upon or neer the Cusp of the second house, then Loss was at hand to the Merchant
If Sun being in the sixt house, disposed by Venus, and he Retrograde in the second, not beholding Sun, then Loss was at hand to the Merchant
If Jupiter also calling her sinister square to the Moon and and Mars his Dexter square, then Loss was at hand to the Merchant
If Principall Signisicators under the Earth, then Ill, worst of all, if in the fourth, for that is an assured testimony of sinking the Ship
If The hour when the astrologer breaks open a letter and perceives the intention of the Querent., then The true time to erect the figure and draw astrological judgment.

If The mind of the Astrologer is heavily perplexed with the success of any matter wherein himself is really concerned., then He may accept of that hour for the true time of erecting his scheme of Heaven.
If Ascendant, its lord, and the Moon are significators., then Used to determine if the querent will be rich.
If Planets are all angular., then A good sign of substance.
If Planets are in succedent houses, direct and swift in motion., then A good sign of substance.
If Planets are in good houses, direct, and moderately dignified in essential dignities., then A hopeful argument of an estate.

If Lord of the Ascendant and the Moon are corporally joined together with the lord of the second house., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If Lord of the Ascendant and the Moon have friendly aspect to the Lord of the second., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If Jupiter or Venus cast their sextile or trine to the Part of Fortune., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If Jupiter or Venus are in conjunction with the Part of Fortune., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If Lord of the second is in the Ascendant., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If The Moon is in the second house., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If Lord of the Ascendant is in the second house., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If Any planet transfers the light and virtue of the Lord of the second to the Lord of the Ascendant., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If Benevolent planets cast their sextile or trine to the cusp of the Ascendant or Part of Fortune., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If A fixed star of the nature of Jupiter and Venus ascends with the cusp of the second house., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If A fixed star of the nature of Jupiter and Venus is in conjunction with the Part of Fortune., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If Jupiter, who is natural significator of substance, is in the second house and no infortune casts his aspect unto them., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.

If Venus, who is naturally a fortune, is in the second house and no infortune casts his aspect unto them., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If All the planets are direct and swift in motion., then The querent shall not scare poverty, for he will be competently rich or have a sufficient fortune to subsist on.
If Lord of the second house is in the second house., then The querent shall obtain an estate by his own labor and proper industry.
If Lord of the second is placed in the Ascendant., then He shall unexpectedly come to a fortune or without much labor attain it.
If Lord of the second or the Moon promise substance by any aspect they have to each other., then Consider from what house the aspect is, or of what house the Moon is Lady of.
If Neither the Lord of the second nor the Moon promise substance., then See to the Part of Fortune what house it is in, and what house of Heaven the dispositor is Lord of.
If The planet assisting or promising increase of fortune is Lord of the Ascendant., then The Querent himself will by his own diligence advance his own Fortune.

If The planet assisting or promising increase of fortune is Lord of the Ascendant and he is a mean man or mechanical., then Then by the sweat and labor of his own hands, his own invention, care and pains-taking shall put him into a Fortune.

If The adjuvant planet is Lord of the second., then He will augment his estate by advance of his own stock, and well managing his private fortune, and adventuring to buy and sell in such things as naturally he is addicted unto, or falls in his way in the course of his life, or are of the nature of that planet.

If The Lord of the third fortunate the Lord of the second, or the cusp of the house, or Part of Fortune., then He will be assisted in procuring an estate by some honest neighbor, or some one of his kindred, brethren or sisters, if he have any, or by some journey he shall undertake, or removing to that quarter of Heaven from whence the Lord of the third casts his good aspect, or is corporally joined with the significator of substance.

If The fortunate planet or significator is Lord of the fourth, or placed in the fourth., then The Querent will attain wealth by means of his father's diligence, (if he be living) or by some aged person, as grandfather, andc. or by taking some farme or grounds, or purchase of tenements, lands or hereditaments., or well managing the stock his ancestors have or shall leave him, or some stock of money his kindred may lend him.
If The Lord of the fifth does promise wealth and the querent is a gentleman., then He obtains means by play, cards, dice, sports, pastimes.
If The Lord of the fifth does promise wealth and the querent is of capacity, and a courtier., then He obtains means by some embassage, message, andc.

If The Lord of the fifth does promise wealth and the querent is an ordinary man., then He obtains means by keeping a victualling house, as ale-house, inne, taverne, bowling-alley, or being a door-keeper, porter to some gentleman.
If The Lord of the fifth house is strong., then He promises somewhat out of the estate of the father, or by making matches, 8cc.

If The Lord of the sixth, or significator, or assistant planet is in the sixth, and the sign of the sixth is humane and the querent is a king or prince., then His subjects will assist him with many tumbling subsidies, privy-seales, loanes of money, Sec.

If The Lord of the sixth, or significator, or assistant planet is in the sixth, and the sign of the sixth is humane and the querent is a nobleman or gentleman., then He shall augment his estate by letting leases, and the discreet managing of his estate by his stewards, bailiffs, and such as undertake for him.

If The Lord of the sixth, or significator, or assistant planet is in the sixth, and the sign of the sixth is humane and the querent is a country-man, as a farmer or husband-man., then Tell him he may thrive by dealing in little cattle, as sheep, goats, hogs, conies, Sec.

If The Lord of the sixth, or significator, or assistant planet is in the sixth, and the sign of the sixth is humane and the querent is a scholar., then Advise him to turne physician, for he shall thrive by his salary obtained from people infirm and diseased.

If The Lord of the seventh house fortunate the Lord of the second, or the cusp of the house, or the Part of Fortune., then Let the Querent expect Gods blessing, by means of a rich and good wife, or the assistance of some loving woman.

If The Lord of the seventh house fortunate the Lord of the second, or the cusp of the house, or the Part of Fortune and the querent is a gentleman., then Then by the sword, or the wars,’or by law recovering somewhat detained from him, by contracting of bargains, by the common acquaintance he hath in his way of trade of commerce? if he be a merchant.

If The Lord of the eighth is that planet who fortunates the significators above named., then The Querent shall either have some legacy bequeathed him by testament of a deceased party, or a further increase of his wife's portion, little by him expected at time of his question, or shall goe uncompelled, and reside in some country, where increase of substance shall happen unto him, viz, he shall unexpectedly settle himself where formerly he had no intention, and there shall thrive and grow rich.

If The Lord of the ninth give virtue or fortunate the Part of Fortune or Lord of the second, or cusp of the House., then The Querent may thrive by some voyage to sea, if Jupiter or Mercury descend by the cusp of the ninth, and the Lord of the same Signe be therein, or one of his wives brothers, or some allied unto her, or a neer Neighbour, to the place where she did live when he first married her, or some religious man or Minister shall befriend him in the way of his Vocation or Calling, for the increase of his Fortune.

If An Earthly Signe be on the cusp of the ninth, and the Lord of that house, then The Querent shall thrive by some secret plot, or by some treasure hid in the earth, or by some old house ready to fall, or by some old record or writing, or by some old man that shall instruct him in some secret way to obtain wealth.

If 25 or H descend blithe cusp of the ninth, and the Lord of the same Signe he therein, or one of his Wives Brothers, or some allied unto her, ora neer Nighbour, to the place where (he did live whesi he sirft married her, or some religious man orMiniiter lhall befriend him in rhe way of his Vocation or Calling, then thrive by some Voyage to Sea, for the encrease of his Fortune

If an Earthly Signe be on the cufp of the ninth, and the Lord of that house be therein polked, then thrive by removing to that part of Heaven, or that- Coaft of the Kingdome or County signisied by the Signe and quarter of Heaven, and by dealing in the native Commodities of that County, City or Coun¬ try to which the Heavens direft him
If the Lord of the second be sortunate in the tenth house, then augment his estate orget asubfiftence by the lervice or imploymenc of lonie King, Prince, Nobleman, Gentleman, Ma- ,fter, or the like
If the Lord of the tenth and second be in reception, then augment his estate orget asubfiftence by the lervice or imploymenc of lonie King, Prince, Nobleman, Gentleman, Ma- ,fter, or the like

If the Lord ofthe tenth doe behold the Lord of the second or Cufpe of the second house, then augment his estate orget asubfiftence by the lervice or imploymenc of lonie King, Prince, Nobleman, Gentleman, Ma- ,fter, or the like
If the Sun with any benevolent configuration, then augment his estate orget asubfiftence by the lervice or imploymenc of lonie King, Prince, Nobleman, Gentleman, Ma- ,fter, or the like

If one inquires that is young and of fmall . sor¬ tune, then Iearne a Mechanicall trade, according to thenature ofthe signe of the tenth and Planet who is Lord thereof ; /or the heavens intimate he (hall doe! well in his Magillery or Trade, if he be capable and sit sor it
If a man of any education and desirous of preserment, then peft an Office or Publique imployment in the Common- weal

If theXord of the eleventh be that benevolent Planet who is li<misicator in the premises, viz. the Planet sortunating, then some /riend (lull commend the party inquiring to accept nf some imployment very advantagious, or some Merchant, Courtier, orfervantof a Nobleman, King, or great perfon ftall be the meanes of railing the to a Fortun-, and then things lhall unexpeftedly happen unto the querent which i.„ n’ver thought of.: and this sor good
If the Fortunate Planet, who calls his Afpeft as asorelaid,be in the twelfth, then the querent (hall advance his Fortune by great Cattle Horfe-raceSj by imprisonments, or men lmpriioned
If the Lord of the sirft and (ccond and Jupiter be jay ned together in the second House, sirft, tenth, (eventh, sourth or eleventh, then the querent (hall be rich and continue so
