If Moon applies to the lord of the ascendant with a good aspect, then the Fugitive shall returne and be recovered or shall be so hindered that he shall come againe

If the lord of the seventh or the Moon separate from the Fortunes and be immediately conjoined to the Infortunes, then the Fugitive shall returne and be recovered or shall be so hindered that he shall come againe
If Moon is increasing in light and number, then he shall be long in search
If Moon is decreasing, then soon sound and with less labour
If Moon separating from the Lord of the seventh and joined with the Lord of the ascendant, then the Fugitive is sorry he went and will send some to entreat for him
If Lord of the seventh combust, then the Fugitive will be taken will he nill he
If Lord of the seventh joined to an Infortune in an angle, then upon good search the Fugitive will be taken
If Lord of the seventh joined to an Infortune not in an angle, then he shall be detained or stayed by the way but not imprisoned

If Lord of the ascendant behold that Infortune who afflicts the Fugitive, then the querent shall find the Fugitive detained by some one to whom he ought to give money or who will demand money before he do restore the Fugitive unto him
If Infortune in the ninth house, then he shall be stayed in his journey and taken
If Lord of the seventh with a Planet stationary, in his first or second station in an angle or succedant, then he knoweth not which way to fly but shall be taken
If Lord of the seventh in the ascendant, then the Fugitive will returne of his owne accord
If Moon separating from the Lord of the ascendant and joined immediately to the Lord of the seventh house or to the seventh house, then one will shortly bring newes of him
If Lord of the seventh combust or entering combustion, then the Fugitive shall be sound (volens -> nolens ; )
If Moon separating from the Lord of the seventh and joined immediately to the ascendant or Lord thereof, then the Fugitive repenteth his departure and will send some to entreat for him
If Moon joined to Infortunes, then he shall be sound or come againe and hath endured much misery since his departure
If Lord of the seventh beholding an Infortune, then the querent shall find him that is fled with some to whom he must give money before he can have him
If Moon separating from Saturn or Mars, then he shall quickly come back againe, or, a thing lost shall suddenly be sound
If Moon ascending her own house with a conjunction or trine, then the Fugitive returneth within three dayes
If Moon ascending her own house with a conjunction or trine, then the querent shall hear where he is within three dayes, it the distance be not too great
If Venus under the earth, Venus Occidental and Retrograde, then she will returns of her owns will
If Venus Oriental, then she cometh, but not willingly
If Lord of the ascendant, the Moon, and Lord of the seventh in trine or sextile, then Ike returneth
If Lord of the ascendant, the Moon, and Lord of the seventh in square or opposition without Reception, then never
If Mars in an angle, and giving the Moon strength, and the ascendant movable, then they shall be contented to be separated for ever

If Lord of the ascendant draws towards the Lord of the seventh, or to the Lord of the hour, or be in the seventh, then the Thief shall be taken anon after, or it gives hopes of discovery of the thing lost

If the lord of the second and the Moon be in the seventh, in the Signe of the seventh, and the Lord of the seventh house behold them both by a conjunction or aspect, then the Goods taken away by somebody

If Moon be Lady of the second, and in the house of the lord of the hour going to conjunction of the lord of the seventh house, then the party lost the thing or Goods in some place where he was and hath forgot it, and it is neither lost nor stolen, but carelessly mislaid

If Moon be Lady of the ascendant, and in the fourth, and the Lord of the second in the seventh, or in the sign of the eight house, in opposition to the second house, at a trine or sextile to the Moon, then the thing is not stolen, but taken away in jest

If Moon be Lady of the ascendant, and in the ascendant, not farre remote, and the Sun Lord of the second in the tenth with the Lord of the seventh house, and the Lord of the seventh oppresse the Moon with a square, then the Goods stolen and taken away

If Moon be in the third, oppressed with the Lord of the seventh house by his square aspect, and Lord of the second also being Lord of the ascendant, and in the seventh, in the Signe of the Seventh, then it is stolen, but first it was taken in jest, and it will be hard to get it againe, except the Sun and Venus behold them
If Lord of the second in the tenth with the Lord of the seventh house, and the Moon oppresses with a square, then Goods are stolen and taken away

If Moon in the third, oppressed with the Lord of the seventh house by his square aspect, and Lord of the second also being Lord of the ascendant, and in the seventh, in the Sign of the Seventh, then Goods are stolen, but it was taken in jest, and it will be hard to get it again, except the Sun and Venus behold the matter of the question
If Moon in the seventh in the Sign of the Lord of the hour, the Lord of the hour being Lord of the seventh, then Goods are not stolen or taken away, but overlooked and mistaken

If Jupiter in the first house and in Virgo, and is Lady of the hour, and Venus Lady of the second in the tenth, in the Sign of the tenth, and Moon in opposition to the Lord of the seventh, then The party lost the Goods as he went by the way, or was in some place where he left them

If Moon Lady of the hour, in Sagittarius, in the eighth, and the Lord of the second in the first, and neither of them behold the Lord of the seventh, but the Lord of the seventh be in the seventh, then Goods are taken away in jest by the Master of the house, and he will deny it

If Moon lady of the hour in the fourth, in conjunction to the lord of the seventh, and the lord of the second in the twelfth, in a conjunction to the lord of the the seventh, then Somebody has taken the things away in jest
If Moon in the Sign of the lord of the seventh, and not beholding the lord of the seventh, but Moon in the twelfth, and lord of the second in the sixth, then Goods are taken away in jest
If Lord of the second did last separate from the lord of the house of the Moon, then Goods is stolen in jest, but will scant be had again
If Moon separates from the lord of the second by quartile, then Goods is taken away and stolen by somebody
If Moon is lady of the second, and does separate from the lord of the house wherein she is, then It is stolen
If Lord of the ascendant does separate from Saturn, or from the lord of the second house, then Querent lay it down and forgot it, and so it was lost
If Lord of the ascendant and lord of the second do separate from Saturn, then It is the surer
If Moon is Lady of the ascendant, and separates from Saturn, and does apply unto the lord of the second house, which did also last separate from Saturn, then Judgment as aforesaid
If Lord of the ascendant, as Sun is also lord of the second, and does separate from Saturn, then Judgment as aforesaid

If Lord of the second or Saturn doe separate from the lord of the ascendant, then Party lost the Goods by the way as he went, or in some place where he was, or else it tumbled out of his pocket or fell into some secret place where it is not stolen or found
If Peregrine Planet or lord of the seventh or Mars who is also for the Thief doe apply to Saturn or the lord of the second, then Goods absolutely stolen and the Thief came with intent for to steal

If Lord of the second or Saturn doe apply unto the peregrine Planet or to the lord of the seventh or to Mars who is for the Thief, then Goods or the thing lost did offer itself to the Thief or he came easily by them without trouble, for he that stole them, came not with intent for to have stolen it but seeing the thing did lye so open and so carelessly he took it and carried it away

If Venus is lady of the ascendant and also lady of the second and be in and apply by conjunction to the Sun within one degree and Sun be lord of the third house and and be the peregrine Planet and in the tenth and Jupiter apply to Venus and Mars, none of the abovesaid separations or applications impeding or the lord of the seventh in the third, then Querent did lose the thing by the way as he wept and it is not stolen from him
If Signifier of the Thief be in the ascendant or give his virtue to the Moon or the Moon to him, then It is stolen
If Lord of the ascendant give his virtue to the Signifier of the Thief, then It is stolen

If Signifier behold the lord of the ascendant by square or conjunction or the Moon by conjunction square quartile, or the lord of the house of the Moon; or the lord of the Terms of the Moon or the lord of the second house or the Sun or his lord, then The thing is stolen
If Any Planet be in the ascendant and give his power to the Signifier of the Thief or the Significator to him by square or conjunction, then It is stolen

If Infortune in the ascendant or second house or the Lord of the house of the Moon or of the Terme of the Moon is unfortunate or the Sun or his lord be unfortunate or the lord of the ascendant or the lord of the second house be infortunate, then All these signifie loss or losing
If Any Planet be in the ascendant peregrine, then It is stolen
If Peregrine Planet give virtue to the Moon, or the Moon to him, then It is stolen
If Lord of ascendant peregrine, then It is stolen
If Thief be peregrine, that is, if he have no dignities where he is, then It is stolen
If Significator be with the Lord of the ascendant or in square or conjunction to the Lord of the ascendant, then It is stolen
If Any Planet doe separate from the Lord of the house of the Moon, then It is stolen
If Any Planet have respect to the Lord of the Terme of the Moon, with conjunction square or quartile, then It is stolen
If Any Planet be separate from the Lord of the house of Substance, then It is taken away
If Thief have respect unto the Lord of the house of the Moon, with conjunction opposition or quartile, then It is taken away
If Neither the Lord of the house of the Moon or lord of the second separate not themselves from one another, of any other Planet from them, then What you look for is in his owne place
If Moon give virtue to Saturn or Mars, or to any Planet in cadent houses, or to the lord of the eighth, then Not stolen, but missing, or else negligently thrown aside

If Moon be lady of the seventh, and give her virtue to a Planet in the second, or in the eleventh or first, having herself neither conjunction or trine to the cusps of the houses, then It will be, or is intended to be stolen
If Any Planet in the seventh give virtue to a Planet in the second, sixth or eleventh, and have no conjunction or trine to the Planet in the seventh, then It will be, or is intended to be stolen
If Lord of the tenth be in conjunction square or quartile with the Thief, then It will be stolen
If A Planet doe separate himself from the lord of the house of the Moon or from the Lord of the second, then It is taken away with hands and stolen
If Moon be Lady of the seventh, and give vertue to the Lord of the ascendant, then It is stolen
If Lord of the ascendant give vetue to the Moon in the seventh, then It is stolen
If Any Planet in the ascendant give vertue to the Signifier of the Thief, then It is stolen

If Thief to the Lord of the ascendant, then It's stolen, but the Thief gives so much of the Goods to the owner againe, according to the vertue or light that the Thief giveth to the Lord of the ascendant
If Any Planet in the ascendant be peregrinee, then It is stolen, and the Thief shall escape
If Peregrinee planet give vertue to the Moon, or the Moon to him, then It is stolen
If Thief aspect the Moon with conjunction, square or quartile, or aspect the Lord of the Terme of the Moon, then It is stolen

If Moon give vertue to Saturn or Mars. or if she give vertue to any Planet in a cadent house, or if the Moon give vertue to the Lord of the eighth, and he in a movable Signe, then The things are stolen
If Moon give vertue to the Lord of the eighth, and he in a fixed Signe, then Things are taken away
If Lord of the house of the Moon separate from any Planet, or the Lord of the second doe separate from any Planet, then Stolen
If Lord of the house of the Moon or second be in his owne house, and have vertue of Saturn or Mars, then Gone away, by itselfe, and not stolen

If Sun and Moon beholding the attendant or the Lord of the attendant in the first house, or joined to the Lord of the seventh house, then The thief is one of the house, or one that frequents the house
If The Luminaries in their proper houses, or in the house of the Lord of the attendant, then The thief is one of the house, or one that frequents the house
If The Luminaries in the Triplicity of the Lord of the attendant, then The thief is a neighbor
If The Luminaries in the Terms of the Lord of the attendant, then The thief is a familiar
If Moon in the ninth house in square or opposition to Saturn or Mars, then The chart brings back the thief
If Moon in the ninth house in square or opposition to Saturn or Mars, and they are retrograde, then The chart brings back the thief without fail
If Sun and Moon aspect the Lord of the attendant, and not the ascendant, then The thief is known to the owner
If Significator of the Thief strong in the attendant, then The thief is a brother or kinsman
If Saturn, Lord of the seventh house, in the ninth house from his own house, then The thief is a stranger
If Sun and Moon beholding each other, then The thief is a kinsman
If Lord of the attendant in the third or fourth house, then Accuses thine own household servant

If Lord of the seventh in the ascendant or fourth house, then The thief is one of the house, or of the household, or frequenting the house, and is in the City or Town, and is one whom the querent least mistrusteth, and one which will hardly confess the fact
If Lord of the seventh in the second house in a masculine sign, then The thief is one of the household or an acquaintance

If Lord of the seventh in the second house in a feminine sign, then The thief is his Wife, perhaps a Sweetheart or Maid of the house, and is within the power of the Loser, or some of his house, and may be recovered by money

If Lord of the seventh in the third house, then The thief is one of the Kindred, Brothers, Sisters, Cozens, or his only Fellow by way of service, or some Neighbour often in his sight, or his Disciple, Messenger or Servant

If Lord of the seventh in the fourth house, then The thief is his Father, or some old Body, or of his Fathers Kin, or one dwelling in the Heritage or house of his Father, and the Thief hath given it to his Wife, or the woman to her Husband, or it is the good man or good Wife of the house, or else he is a Tiller or Labourer of the Land for the querent

If Lord of the seventh in the fifth house, then The thief is the Sonne or Daughter of him, or the Sonne or Daughter of his Cozen or Nephew (if the Sign be a masculine) or of the household of his Father, or else his very Friend

If Lord of the seventh in the sixth house, then The thief is a Servant, a Disciple or Labourer to the querent, or one conversant with some Churchman, a Brother or Sifter of the Father, a sick body or unsteadfast, or grieved person
If Lord of the seventh in the seventh house, then The thief is his Wife or Lady, or an Harlot, or a woman that useth to be suspected for such matters, or a Buyer or Seller in Markets

If Lord of the seventh in the seventh house in a feminine sign, then The Taker is an utter enemy to the Loser, by some cause formerly happened between them, and dwels somewhat far from him, and the things are in his custody still, and hard to be recovered

If Lord of the seventh in the eighth house, then The thief is a Stranger, yet seemeth to be one of the household, or one of his open enemies or of his neer Kinswoman for some cause of offence done or some evil disposed person (and of the Livery of the Man) and he useth to come to his House and either is kept by him or else doth some servile acts as a Butcher or Labourer doth otherwhiles to kill Cattell and it seemeth the thing lost will not be had againe but by either faire words, or dread of death or by reason of some threats or else the thing is lost by some Man absent the which is not now had in minde at this timebut seemt to be quite sorgotten

If Lord of the seventh in the ninth house, then The thief is an honest person a Clarke or a Church-man and the Thief is out of the way or Country a Disciple or Governour to some Mafter of some priviledged Place or a poore vagrant person hard to be recovered but by some religious person as asoresaid

If Lord of the seventh in the tenth house, then The thief is A Lord or Mafter or Governour in the Kings house or of his Household ; or some Lady or Gentlewoman if the Figure be Feminine, ande centra ; or some crafts-Mafter , usually its some person that lives handsomely and is not neceflitated to this course of lise

If Lord of the seventh in the eleventh house, then The thief is a Friend or one knowne by some service done ; or of the household of some man of the Church or Neighbour or servant in the place where the Querent hath some Lordship and is put in truft, or is of the Household of the Querent his Mother and by such a one or his meanes to be recovered againe

If Lord of the seventh in the twelfth house, then The thief is a Stranger, envious a false person and inthralled, incumbred or oppreffed with poverty and hath no riches ; wheresore he hath visited many Regions as some Enemy or Beggar dothand he joyeth in it ; judge his quality by the Signe and Place and commix all these with the other testinionies of the Signes and Planets
If Significator of the Thief in head of a Sign direct or separating from Combustion or applying to a Planet in the third or ninth House, then The thief is gone or going but of the Town
If The removing of the Significator out of one Signe into another, then Denotes change of Lodging or removing
If Significator is a superior Planet, then The rule is infallible
If Lord of the ascendant and the Moon are not in one quarter but above ninety degrees asunder, then Notes departure and a great distance betwixt the Goods and the Owner
If Lord of the ascendant and the Moon are in angles, and applying to Planets in angles, then Notes no far distance
If Moon and the Lord of the ascendant are in one quarter, then Notes no far distance
If Significator of the Thief is in a fixed Sign, then Account for every house betwixt the Lord of the ascendant and him, three miles
If Significator of the Thief is in a common Sign, then Account for every house betwixt the ascendant and Thief, one mile
If Significator of the Thief is in a movable Sign, then Account for every house betwixt the ascendant and the Thief, that so many houses on the earth are betwixt the Loser and the Thief
If Sign ascending is a fixed Sign, then For every house give three miles
If Sign ascending is a common Sign, then For every house give one mile
If Sign ascending is a movable Sign, then For every house reckon one half mile
If Significator is in an angle, then The thief is still in the Town
If Significator is in a succedant house, then The thief is not far off
If Significator is in a cadent house, then The thief is far gone
If Significator of the Thief in an angle, then The thief is in a house
If Moon in an angle, then The thief is in his own house
If Significator in a succedant house, then The thief is in Closes
If Moon in a succedant house, then The thief is in his own Closes
If Significator of the Thief in a cadent house, then The thief is in a Common
If Moon in a cadent house, then The thief is in his own Common, or that which belongs to the Town he lives in
If Signifier of the Thief is within thirty degrees of the Lord of the ascendant, then The thief is near him that lost the Goods
If Signifier of the Thief is within seventy degrees of the Lord of the ascendant, then The thief is within the Town or Parish of him that lost the Goods
If The more degrees betwixt the Signifier of the Thief and the Lord of the ascendant, then The farther off they are from each other
If Significator is in a square aspect to the Lord of the ascendant, then The thief is out of the Town
If Lord of the seventh is strong, and in an angle, then The Thief is not yet gone out of the Town or Parish where the Theft was acted
If Lord of the seventh is found weak in an angle, then The thief is gone, or departing
If Lord of the seventh is in the ascendant, then The Thief will be at home (before him) or before he get home
If Lord of the seventh is in the seventh, then The thief is hid at home and dare not be seen
If Lord of the sixth is in the first or second with any of their Lords, then The Thief is of the house of the Querent
If Lord of the ascendant and the Significator of the Thief be together, then The Thief is with the Querent
If Planet that signifies his going out of Town is in a fiery Sign, then The thief is in the east part of the Town or Country
If Planet that signifies his going out of Town is in a watery Sign, then The thief is in the north part of the Town or Country
If Planet that signifies his going out of Town is in an airy Sign, then The thief is in the west part of the Town or Country
If Planet that signifies his going out of Town is in an earthly Sign, then The thief is in the south part of the Town or Country
If Significator of the Thief is in a fiery Sign, then The thief went east
If Significator of the Thief is in an earthly Sign, then The thief went south
If Significator of the Thief is in an airy Sign, then The thief went west
If Significator of the Thief is in a watery Sign, then The thief went north
If Significator of the thief in a fiery sign, then The thief went east
If Significator of the thief in an earthly sign, then The thief went south
If Significator of the thief in an airy sign, then The thief went west
If Significator of the thief in a watery sign, then The thief went north
If Lord of the seventh in Aries, then Eastward
If Lord of the seventh in Taurus, then South against the east
If Significator of the thief in the ascendant, then The thing is in the point of the east
If Significator of the thief in the seventh house, then The thing is just in the west
If Significator of the thief in the fourth house, then The thing is just in the northern
If Significator of the thief in the tenth house, then The thing is south
If Sun in an oriental sign, then The house is in the east part from the master or from him that lost the goods
If Moon in a fixed sign, then The house has but one door
If Moon in a movable sign, then The door is high above the earth, and it may be there is one other little one
If Saturn has any aspect to the sign the Moon is in, then The door has been broken and after mended again, or else it is black or very old
If Mars has any aspect to the sign the Moon is in, then The gate or door shall have some token of burning or fire
If Saturn and Jupiter have a friendly aspect to the same sign, then The gate is iron, or most part of it, or a good strong one
If Moon is infortunate, then The gate or door is broken or brustled
If Moon has small lights, then The house has no door opening to the high-way, but opens on the back part of the house
If Moon in square or opposition to Mars, then The door is burned with iron, fire or candle, or has been cut with some iron instrument
If Moon in trine or sextile to Saturn, then The door of the thief's house is mended with iron
If Moon is but newly increased in light, then His gate or door is part under the earth, or under a bank-side, or they go down by a step
If Moon in a fixed or movable sign, then He has but one door outwardly
If Moon in common signs, then More than one door
If Moon in Taurus or Virgo, then The gate is under the earth, or the house standeth on the bank side
If Moon in movable signs, then The gate or door is above the earth, and a step to go up into it, or one ascends somewhat in going into the house
If Moon infortunate, then The gate is broken
If Saturn aspects the Moon with square or opposition, then The door or gate is broken down, old or black
If Saturn aspects the Moon with trine or sextile, then The door is mended again
If Moon is fixed, then The house has but one door, near to the ground
If Moon is in a movable sign, then The gate is up some steps
If Saturn beholds the sign, then The gate is, or has been broken, and is very ancient or is black
If Mars beholds the sign, then It is rent or cracked, or torn, or needs repair
If Mars beholds the sign and the Moon has but small light, then There is no great appearance of iron work
If Lord of the Ascendant in an angle, then The goods are in his hands
If Lord of the hour in an angle, then The goods are in his hands
If Lord of the house of the Moon with the Lord of the hour in an angle, then The goods are in his hands, and are goods movable
If Lord of the hour and the Lord of the term of the Moon and the Lord of the second in an angle with the Lord of the Ascendant, then They are in his hands and fixed goods
If Any of these lords in an angle, with square, opposition, or trine to the Lord of the Ascendant, then The owner shall have his goods again
If Lord of the Ascendant and Lord of the hour in a succedent house, then The goods are about the owner
If Lord of the house of the Moon in a movable sign, then They are not far from the owner
If Lord of the term of the Moon, or the Lord of the second in a succedent house, then Then the things are about the owner, and not much elongated
If The planets last before spoken of, or rehearsed, placed in cadent houses, then Show the goods far from the owner
If Signifier of the thief gives their power to another planet, then The things stolen are not in the keeping of the thief
If Signifier of the thief does not give their power to another planet, then It remains in his own power, custody or possession
If Infortunate planet in a fortunate term, then He was of a vile stock, and now is in good state
If Fortune in the term of an infortune, then Say the contrary
If Lord of the seventh in an angle, then He was willing to have carried all away, but could not
If Lord of the seventh in a succedent house, and the Lord of the eighth with him strong, then He had all
If Both the Lord of the seventh and eighth in cadent houses, then He neither carried it away or had it
If Signs are fixed, then Account for every degree a mile
If Signs are common, then So many tenths of miles
If Signs are movable, then So many rods
If Lord of the Ascendant in a fixed sign, then Give for every sign four miles
If Lord of the Ascendant in a common sign, then Give for every sign a mile and a half
If Lord of the Ascendant in a movable sign, then Give for every sign half a mile
If Significator in Aries, then It is in a place where beasts do use that be small, as sheep, or hogs
If Significator in Taurus, then It is in a place of beasts which bite as dogs
If Significator in Gemini, then It is in a place of great beasts that are ridden, as in a stable of horses, or such like
If Significator in Cancer or Leo, then It signified a house or place of great beasts, as oxen, kine, or such other cattle
If Significator in Virgo or Libra, then Signifieth a place of camels, mules, horses, asses, and such like
If Significator in Virgo, then Hath the signification of a barn, and of such places as be under the earth, or near to the earth, or granaries, such as they put corn in
If Significator in Libra, then Signifieth a place of goats, sheep, hogs, and such like
If Significator in Scorpio or Sagittarius, then It is in the house
If Significator in Scorpio, then It is in the wall of the house
If Significator in Sagittarius, then Near a little house or closet
If Significator in Capricorn, then It is near a door that is above a door or gate, in some place on high
If Lord of the fourth in a moveable Sign, then the thing is in an high place
If Lord of the fourth in a fixed Sign, then the thing is on the Earth
If Lord of the fourth in a common Sign, then the thing is in a covered place
If Lord of the second and Lord of the fourth in one Sign, then the things are where they are, and the Thiese and Theft both together
If Lord of the fourth in a fixed Sign, then the Goods are in the Earth, or in a House having no Chamber
If Lord of the fourth in a moveable Sign, then the Goods are in a Chamber above another, or in an upper Loft or Room
If Lord of the fourth in a common Sign, then the Goods are in a Chamber within another Chamber
If Goods in a fiery Sign, then they are East
If Goods in an Earthy Sign, then they are South
If Goods in an Airy Sign, then they are West
If Goods in a Watery Sign, then they are North
If Lord of the term of the Moon in an angle and in a moveable Sign, then the Goods are in Closes where are both Corne and Grafle
If Lord of the term of the Moon in a succedent and fixed Sign, then in Woods, Parkes, or in closed Grounds that lyeth from the High-way- side
If Lord of the term of the Moon in a cadent and common Sign, then in a Common of divers Mens, or Pasture or Meddow of divers Mens
If Saturn in the Ascendant, then the thing was under a Bed, neer a Robe or Covering
If Lord of the fourth is Saturn, then it is hid in a dark place or place every part of the house, or in a desolate or stinking place and deep, be it a siege-house or Jakes, where people seldom come
If Lord of the fourth is Jupiter, then it signifies a place of Wood, Bushes or Bryers
If Lord of the fourth is Mars, then it is in some Kitchen, or in a place where fire is used, or in a Shop
If Lord of the fourth is Sun, then it signifieth the Cloyster or Hall of the house, or the Place or Seat of the Master of the house
If Lord of the fourth is Venus, then it signifieth the place of the Seat of a woman, or Bed, or Cloathes, or where women are most conversant
If Lord of the fourth is Mercury, then it is in a place of Piandures, Carving or Books, or a place of Corne, and chiefly in fra
If Lord of the fourth is Moon, then it is in a Pit, Cifterne or Lavatory
If Mercury in a common Sign, then it is in a little Cell within another Chamber
If Mercury in a movable Sign, then it is within a little Cell that hath another Chamber about it
If Mercury in a fixed Sign, then it is in a house that hath no Sellar nor other Chamber, as many Country-houses have not
If Jupiter or Venus in the tenth house, then the door hath a faire opening
If Saturn in the tenth house, then the opening of the door is neer some Ditch or Pit, or deep place
If Mars in the tenth house, then neer to the opening of the house is the place of making a fire, or killing of Beasts, or heading
If Mercury in the tenth house, then in the opening of the house, is a place where the Master of the house keeps his things in, viz. his instruments or Tools he uses about his Beasts
If Sun in the tenth house, then in the opening is some Stoole or Seat to sit on, or a bed

If Moon in the tenth house, then in the entring of the house is a door under the ground, or some other necessary thing that a man hath much occasion to use in his house, as a Furnace or Quern, or such like
If Moon in the seventh aspecting the Lord of the Ascendant with a trine, then recovery
If Lord of the second in the Ascendant, then recovery
If Venus in the second direct, then recovery
If Lady of the second in the Ascendant, then recovery
If Moon in the tenth in trine to a Planet in the second, then recovery
If Moon in the second, with a trine to the Lord of the second, then recovery
If Moon in the second, to a square of Sun in the twelfth, then recovery
If Lord of the Ascendant in the second, then recovery
If Sun and Moon aspecting each other with a trine, then recovery
If Sun and Moon aspecting the cusp of the second with a trine, then recovery
If Lord of the second in the fourth, or in the House of the Querent, then recovery

If Lord of the term of the Moon, and the Lord of the house of the Moon be increasing both in motion and number, and free from infortunes, then it shall be recovered whole and sound, and nothing diminished thereof
If Lord of the Ascendant applies unto the Lord of the term of the Moon, then recovery
If Lord of the Ascendant applies unto the Lord of the second House, then recovery
If Lord of the term of the Moon applies unto the Lord of the Ascendant, then the Lord of the thing lost shall recover the same
If Moon or Lord of the Ascendant apply unto the Ascendant, then the thing stolne shall be had againe through inquisition and diligent Search
If Moon or Lord of the Ascendant apply unto the Lord of the second House, then the thing stolne shall be had againe through inquisition and diligent Search
If Moon or Lord of the Ascendant apply unto the Lord of the term of the Moon, then the thing stolne shall be had againe through inquisition and diligent Search
If Lord of the House of the Moon, and the Lord of the Terme of the Moon be both diminished in their motion or number, then the more part is lost and shall not be recovered
If Lord of the term of the Moon, and the Lord of the house of the Moon be increasing in number and motion, and safe from ill fortunes, then the thing shall be restored whole and nothing diminished
If those Signifiers be not cadent from angles, then the things shall be soon recovered
If Lord of the ascendant is strong, and with a good Planet, and well disposed, then great testimony of security
If seventh and the Lord thereof are Fortunate, then Party shall return, though not without great erodes and lets
If Moon is applying with the good, then Fortunate
If Evill Planets in the eighth, then Signification of scare, and terrour, or death
If Lord of the Ascendant be with good or good himself, then he shall return safe
If good Planet in the Ascendant, then he shall return safe
If Sun is with the Lord of the Ascendant in any part of the Question, then he may not goe because the Sun burnes him
If Lord of the seventh is with a good Planet, and the Lord of the Ascendant with an evill, then he shall have some impediment in his way, yet shall not dye
If an evill Planet be with the Lord of the first, and a good one in the first, then if he then goe, he shall suffer great loste, but not death; but questionlesse he will be sorely wounded
If Mars be in the first, or with the Lord of the first, then let him not goe because lode will happen unto him by one whom he meeteth
If an ill Planet be with the Lord of the first, and Mars in the Ascendant, or with his Lord, then he shall be wounded with Wood or Stone
If Saturn and Venus or the Moon be in the Ascendant, or with the Lord thereof, or evill Planets in the first, or with the Lord thereof, then he shall receive a vyound,and go neer to dye thereof
If evill Planet be in the eight, then it is to be feared, death will ensue
If the Sun be with the Lord of the seventh, or in the eight, then it is ill to goe
If good Planets be in the first, and malevolent in the seventh and the Lord of the Ascendant good, and Lord of the seventh ill, then the Querent overcomes

If an Infortune be with the Lord of the Ascendant, and an evill Planet in the Ascendant and the Lord of the seventh good,and a good Planet there, then the Querent shall be overcome, and shall be taken or slaine

If both the Lord of the Ascendant and seventh be in the Ascendant,and on the behalfe of the Lord of the Ascendant,there be good Planets calling their benevolent aspects to the cuspe of the second, then the Querent will doe well in the Warre and obtaine money thereby; he shall have victory of his Adversary, or they will endevour to be reconciled

If both the Lords, viz. of the first and seventh, be in the Ascendant, and good Planets be on the part of the Ascendant, and evill on the part of the seventh, then both Parties shall suffer a losse; but the Querent shall have the better in the end
If Lord of the seventh be in the Ascendant, of his Question, then Fortitude of the Ascendant
If Lord of the Ascendant be in the eight, or with the Lord of the eight, or the Lord the eight in the Ascendant, then Que sitted
If Lord of the seventh be In the second, or with the Lord thereof; or the Lord of the second in the seventh, or with the Lord of the seventh, then it notes the death of the Adversary
If Lord of the Ascendant and seventh be in angles, then neither shall overcome
If one is joyned to an evil Planet in a cadent House, then that Party shall be over-come
If both be joyned to Infortunes, then both Parties will be undone by the Suit, or receive infinite prejudice

If one be strong and the other weake, and he that is strong be not cadent, nor joyned to an Infortune , and he that is ill dignifyed, or in a weake quarter of Heaven or House, and he is not in his owne House, or Exaltation, or with a good Planet, then the strongest in the Scheame overcomes
If good Planets be in the seventh and first, then the Society shall be, and good will come of it
If a good Planet be in the seventh, then the Society or Partnerfhip shall be that yeere
If Lord of the Ascendant and seventh agree in nature and quality, then the Parties will agree
If Lord of the Ascendant and seventh disagree in nature and quality, then they will disagree, and there will be perpetuall (or at least often) jangling
If Significator of the querent be in better dignities then the quejted, then the querent prospers
If Significator is in a cadent house, then that party doth the worst
If Significator be exalted, then he gaineth
If the best Planet, or the lord thereof in the best place, or joyned to the best Planet in the second and his Lord, and the eight and his lord, then he shall gaine most
If both second and eight are good, then both shall gaine
If both second and eight are ill, then both shall lose
If one of second and eight is good and the other ill, then he that hath the good Planet shall gaine, the other shall lose
If a good Planet be in the seventh, then then he pretendeth thee or the querent good fellowlhip and meanes really

If the same Planet or the lord of the seventh behold the ascendant,or the lord thereof, with a Trine or Sextile aspect, then it shall endure so many Months, Dayes or Years, as he hath Degrees to goe in the same House, or Signe, sixed, common, or moveable
If fourth and seventh houses, and their lords, and if they be good and strong, and well affeanded,and good Planets in the said houses, then it is very good
If Lord of the ascendant stronger then the lord of the seventh, then abide
If Lord of the ascendant weaker then the lord of the seventh, then remove
If both be evill disposed, then goe
If both indifferent and better aspected of good then evill, then stay
If separating from Infortunes, then goe
If separating from Fortunes, then abide
If Lord of the ascendant separate from Infortunes and apply to Fortunes, then goe
If Lord of the ascendant separate from Fortunes, and applieth to Infortunes, then Stay
If Lord the ascendant and Moon agree, then the judgment is more certain
If Lord of the ascendant or Moon separated from evill Planets, and joyned to Fortunes, then it is better to goe then stay and doe any businesse thou intended
If Lord of the ascendant be separated from fortunes,and apply to infortunes, then neither move or do the businesse thou in tended
If planet to whom the Moon applyes be better then that she separated from, then then thou mayest remove
If Lord of the fourth be in the seventh, and be a good planet, and if the lord of the first and seventh be good , and with good planets, then it is good to abide still
If Lord of the seventh be with a good planet, and the lord of the fourth with an evill one, then it is then not good to stay; for if he doe, he shall receive much dammage by abiding there
If ascendant a signe of sour footed beasts, then hunting
If ascendant an earthly signe, then hunting
If Planet is strong and well-positioned, then The person is the best and most likely to win, and has the best cause.
If More planets in the ascendant and second house, then The querent will have more friends.
If Both significators give their virtue to one planet, then There will be one who will intercede between them.
If Significators of the ascendant and seventh house are fixed, then Both querent and opponent are resolutely bent to proceed in suit or controversy.
If Movable signs are present, then They have no great stomach to the business, but will end it very shortly.
If Common signs are present, then They will continue the suit long, and have the cause out of one court into another.
If The Infortunes are on one's part, then That party shall receive most prejudice, sorrow, and trouble by the contention.
If Moon is joined with the Lord of the seventh, then The querent may then buy the thing or commodity he desires, and this quickly.
If Lord of the ascendant is a more light planet than the Lord of the seventh, then The querent will occasion the sale of it.
If Lord of the seventh is a more light planet than the Lord of the ascendant, then The querent will occasion the sale of it.

If Preceding significators have no aspect to each other, but a planet transfers the light of one to the other, then A friend shall then appear who will drive on the bargain for them both, so that the matter will be done.
If Lord of the seventh is in the ascendant, then The seller will importune the querent to buy.
If Lord of the ascendant is in the seventh, then The querent has most mind to buy.
If Mars or Mercury in the ascendant, then The buyer performs his work suddenly without any labor.
If Sun in the ascendant, and not corporally joined to any other planet, then The buyer performs his work suddenly without any labor.
If Venus or Jupiter in the ascendant, not infected with the evil aspect of an Infortune, then They fortunate the buyer, and he performs what he intended.
If Saturn or Mars in the ascendant, then Argue labor and difficulty, and that the matter will not be had without much labor.
If Mercury in the ascendant, then The buyer is a cunning companion, and means deceitfully, and will deceive the seller if possible.
If Infortunes in the seventh, then Have care of the seller; he will find out one trick or other to delude the buyer; he is a crafty fellow.

If Moon is void of course, unless the significators apply strongly, then There's seldom any bargain concluded, or commodity at that time bought, and yet both parties wrangle, and have some meetings to no purpose.
If Planet from whom the Moon separates enters combustion, then He that sells his land or house at that time shall never recover them again.

If Planet from whom the Moon last separated is free from misfortune, and beholds the Lord of that sign from whence the judgment is required, then It is then possible the seller may in time repurchase the lands or commodities again, or others of as good value.

If Lord of the ascendant and the Moon are in movable signs without reception by house or exaltation, or triplicity or term, then Then there will happen contention, and they will disagree, but matters will again be reconciled, and the partnership will hold out still; they will be mistrustful of one another, nor will much good come of it.

If Significators are in fixed signs, then Their society will continue long, but if no reception be, little profit will from thence accrue to either party; if they buy anything, the commodities will lie long on their hands.
If Significators are in common signs, then It promises a gainful partnership, and that they will be faithful to each other.
If One significator is in a movable sign, and the other in a fixed sign, then The disturbance arising will be less than at first may be feared.
If Ill planets aspect both the significators, viz. Lord of the ascendant and Lord of the seventh, then The partnership will be ill for both, neither the one party or other will deal fairly.
If Moon separates from one Fortune and applies to another, then They will begin well and end well, though neither of them get any wealth.
If Moon is separated from a good planet, and apply to an ill, then They begin well, but end in strife and hatred.
If Moon is separated from an ill planet and apply to another, then They will begin partnership with muttering and repining, continue it with scares and jealousies, end it with law-suits.
If A good planet in the tenth house, then Shows they will obtain reputation, and will rejoice and delight in their mutual society.
If A good planet in the second house, then Best for the querent.
If A good planet in the seventh house, then Best for the partner.
If An ill planet in the second house, then The querent will get little, but be cheated, or entrust much, and get in few debts.
If Lord of the fourth applies to the Lord of the eleventh by sextile or trine, then A good end may be expected by the partnership intended.
If A good planet is in the fourth house, then A good end may be expected by the partnership intended.
If The Lord of the eleventh and fourth are in reception, then A good end may be expected by the partnership intended.
If Good planets cast their sextile or trine to the Lords of the ascendant and seventh, then A good end may be expected by the partnership intended.

If Lord of the seventh or of the eighth cast a square or opposition to the Moon, then The querent must expect no great good from his partner, for it's like he will embezzle the estate, or their common stock.

If Lord of the first house is in the fourth house, strong and fortunate, or joined to the Lord of the fourth in the first, or with the Moon or Lord of the tenth, or in any house except the twelfth, eighth and sixth, conditionally, that the Lord of the first receive the Lord of the fourth, or the Moon receive the Lord of the fourth, though she be not received again, then The town, fort or castle shall be taken.
If Lord of the fourth is in such houses as behold not the fourth (except the Lord of the seventh be in the fourth), then The town, fort or castle shall be taken.
If Lord of the fourth is with the ill Fortunes, and impeded, then It's probable the city shall be taken, and the governor wounded.
If Infortunes are in the fourth without some strong aspect of the Fortunes, then It will be taken, or can it hold out long, or there may be treason in town.

If Mercury is in the fourth, then It will be taken, and there will be some go about to betray or deliver it, or some principal work or fort therein; nor does the governor think himself able to preserve it.

If Lord of the fourth is in the fourth, strong and fortunate, and not retrograde or combust, or besieged of the Infortunes, or if the Lord of the seventh be there, free from all impediments, or if Venus or Jupiter be therein, and no reception between the Lord of the ascendant and fourth, then Then the city, fort or town at that time surrounded or besieged, shall not be taken or delivered to the army now besieging it.

If There is both a Fortune and an Infortune in the fourth, the town shall not be taken, if the Fortune be nearest to the cusp of the house, or first of the two planets which shall transit the degree of the fourth, then The town shall not be taken.
If The Lord of the ascendant is anything weak, or a light planet and unfortunate, then The town shall not be taken.
If Lord of the seventh in the twelfth from the ascendant, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in the twelfth sign from the sign wherein the Lord of the ascendant is, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in the twelfth sign from the sign wherein the Moon is, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh conjunct any planet, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in aspect with a planet who is in opposition to the Lord of the ascendant without reception, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in aspect with a planet who is in square to the Lord of the ascendant without reception, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in aspect with a planet who is in opposition to the Moon without reception, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the seventh in aspect with a planet who is in square to the Moon without reception, then Quesited is averse and an enemy to the Querent
If Lord of the 12th house in square to the Moon with or without Reception, then The Querent has enemies that watch for an opportunity against him
If Lord of the 12th house in opposition to the Moon with or without Reception, then The Querent has enemies that watch for an opportunity against him
If Many planets in the seventh house, then Many enemies
If Many planets in the second house, if they are ill dignified, then Much want of money
If Square aspect, then Shows envy and malice, yet possible to be reconciled
If Opposition aspect without Reception, then Never reconciled
If Venus applying to a conjunction of Saturn, then Small hopes of effecting her desire
If Moon applying to a sextile of Saturn and with Reception between Venus and the Moon, and between Sun and Moon, then Revival of the business and bringing her to her heart's content

If Venus applying to a conjunction of Saturn Lord of the seventh, receiving his virtue which Saturn did render unto him, and he again transferred to the Sun Lord of the ascendant, he meeting with no manner of prohibition, abscission or frustration until his perfect conjunction with the Sun, then No difficulty did afterwards intervene
If Moon is going to a conjunction of Jupiter, then The woman will have the longer life
If Mars afflicts Venus by his presence, then The woman will have the longer life
If Jupiter Lord of his second house in Cancer in his Exaltation, Direct, Swift, and under the Sun-beams, then His Estate is good
If Sun and Venus are applying to Jupiter, then They will well accord
If Mars is ill by nature and vitiated by Saturn, then He will be choleric as well as melancholic
If Mars is ill by nature and vitiated by Saturn, then He will be naturally jealous without cause
If Reception between Jupiter and Moon, then Arguments of a virtuous woman

If A significator in the terms of any planet, then The party varies a little from his natural temper and constitution, retaining a small or great tincture from that planet according as he is dignified.

If Moon applying to a trine of Venus, lord of the ascendant, in an angle, receiving willingly by his retrograde motion her virtue which she brought from Saturn, then The match would suddenly be brought to pass.
If Ascendant and Moon in Sagittarius, together with Jupiter posited in the ascendant, then Signifies a person who was short of stature, corpulent, of a good complexion, and ruddy fresh countenance.
If North latitude of Saturn, then Causes fatness.

If Degrees ascending were in the terms of Jupiter, in an airy sign, and in the face or decanate of the Sun, now posited in a watery sign, and in partile trine to Saturn, both in moist signs, then Argues a phlegmatic, full body.

If Mercury is the significator of the servant, then The servant was a young fellow of about nineteen, a well set fellow, short, big jointed, broad and full faced, dark brown hair, his teeth growing ill-favouredly, a sun-burnt, obscure complexion, yet the skin of his body clear.
If Moon in the second house, in her own house, then The matter should not only recover forthwith his lost goods.
If Moon in the second, and in perfect trine of the Sun in the eleventh, both of them in the midheaven, standing, then The matter should not only recover forthwith his lost goods.
If The nearness of Jupiter to the degree ascending, then The servant was not above three or four houses westward from his master's house.
If Sign of the sixth and his lord, then Signifies the dog.
If Venus, the significator of the dog, is in a western sign but southern quarter of heaven, tending to the west, then The dog ought to be westward from the place where the owner lived.
If Moon is in Sagittarius, a southwest sign, and verging to the western angle, then The dog ought to be westward from the place where the owner lived.

If Venus, significator of the beast, was in a sign of the same triplicity that Cancer his ascendant is, which signifies London, and did apply to a trine of the cusp of the sixth house, then The dog was not out of the lines of communication, but in the same quarter.
If Sign wherein Venus is in, is Gemini, an airy sign, then The dog was in some chamber or upper room, kept privately, or in great secrecy.
If Saturn was under the beams of the Sun and Moon, then The dog was in some chamber or upper room, kept privately, or in great secrecy.
If Moon and Sun were in the eighth house, then The dog was in some chamber or upper room, kept privately, or in great secrecy.

If The Sun on Monday following did apply by dexter trine to Saturn, lord of the ascendant, and Moon to exaltation of Mercury, having exaltation in the ascendant, then He should have his dog again, or news of his dog or small beast upon Monday following, or near that time.
If All fugitives go by the Moon, and as she varies her sign, then The fugitive wavers and shifts in his Highland declines more or less to East, West, North or South.
If A planet who is in an angle or second house, and beholds the seventh house, then The true significator of a thief.
If No peregrine planet be in an angle or the second house, then the lord of the seventh shall be significator of the thief, if he behold the seventh house, then The true significator of a thief.
If Planet to whom the Moon applies, if he behold the seventh house; the rather, if the Moon separate from the lord of the ascendant, then The true significator of a thief.

If A peregrine planet in what angle soever, shall not be significator of the thief, unless he behold the seventh house, or have any dignity in the degree of the seventh, then The true significator of a thief.
If One and the same planet be lord of the hour and of the ascendant, then He shall signify the thief, though he behold not the ascendant.
If A peregrine planet were in the ascendant, then He was significator of the thief.
If Many peregrine planets in angles, many are or may be suspected, justly if they are in opposition or square, not contenting, if in square or opposition, then Many are or may be suspected.
If Mercury in conjunction with Saturn and Mars, then Gave sufficient intimation unto me of the inclination of the querent, who was sufficiently ill conditioned, arrogant, proud, wasteful.
If Venus in Cancer, lately separated or rather in a square of Jupiter, and now almost in partile conjunction with him, then Venus to be significator of the thief.
If Significator of the thief is peregrine, then Thief is peregrine
If Moon in a masculine sign applying to a masculine planet in a masculine sign, then Thief is male
If Mercury signifies youth, then Thief is young
If Saturn in the ascendant, then Thief is deaf
If Venus on the cusp of the 7th house, then The thing lost is not valuable
If Venus in Cancer, then Goods are in a moist sign
If Significator of goods (Mercury) in Cancer, then Goods are in a moist sign
If Moon in Pisces, then Goods are in a moist sign
If Moon in an earthly sign, then Goods are in some low room
If Lord of the house of substance (2nd house) applying to the Moon, then Goods will be heard of again
If Lord of the 2nd house in fall and detriment, then No hope of recovering goods
If Lord of the 2nd house in his own terms and in trine aspect to the Sun, then There is hope of some of the goods
If Significators in watery signs, then Thief lives near water
If Peregrine planet in an angle, then Describes the thief
If Sun or Moon in the ascendant and in essential dignities, then Gives assured hopes of discovering who the thief was
If Application of the Moon to the Lord of the 2nd house, then Argues recovery
If Moon and Lord of the 2nd house essentially dignified, then Full recovery
If Moon and Lord of the 2nd house accidentally fortified, then Partial recovery
If Moon and Lord of the 2nd house apply and are both peregrine, then Discovery, but no recovery
If Jupiter in exaltation and in the 11th house with Venus, Venus being Lady of the ascendant, then All will be well on our side, and the victory ours
If Sun, Lord of our assistants and substance, posited in the 10th house, in the very degree of his Exaltation, then Victory
If Moon applying to Mars and then to Saturn, he angular, then Good news
If Saturn, the Lord of Hopton's significator, is sub radiis, then Enemy should be worsted
If Saturn, the Lord of Hopton's significator, is in his Fall, then Enemy should be worsted
If Saturn, the Lord of Hopton's significator, is in no aspect of any Planet, but wholly peregrine and unfortunate, beholding the cusp of the seventh with a square dexter, then Enemy should be worsted
If General significator of war is friendly to the querent, then Good for the querent in war
If Moon applies to a trine of Saturn in signs of long ascension, then Much difficulty and some fighting
If Saturn and Moon are in reception, then Excellency should obtain and take readings and get glory and honour thereby
If Sun in the seventh, in a fixed sign, then His Majesty would oppose what he could, and send Forces to relieve the Town with all vigour and resolution
If Saturn is better fortified than the Sun, then The King should not prevail
If Jupiter in the fifth, then They wanted not Ammunition
If Lord of the fourth in his Fall with Mars, then To set division amongst the principal Officers, and to incense them against their Officer in Chief
If Mars and Jupiter were not far from each other, then To set division amongst the principal Officers, and to incense them against their Officer in Chief
If Saturn did with his square aspect behold Mars, then To set division amongst the principal Officers, and to incense them against their Officer in Chief
If Sun and Saturn were separated from their aspect, then Excellency would be Master of the Town, yet rather by composition than blood
If Saturn was in like manner separated from the square dexter of Mars from Cardinal Signs, then Excellency would be Master of the Town, yet rather by composition than blood

If Application of the Moon was so directly to the trine of the Lord of the ascendant, without any frustration or prohibition, then Excellency would be Master of the Town, yet rather by composition than blood
If Mars in Taurus with Uranus, then The Governor, was hurt in the head
If Jupiter in the fifth, then They did not want ammunition
If Moon applies to a trine of Saturn, then The querent therefore would have had the victory, by reason of the Verdict given by the Jurors , who ever are signified by the Moon

If Sun is locally in the seventh, opposite to the ascendant, and is Lord of the tenth, then There's no doubt but the Judge would have been averse to the Plaintiff, as his Majesty was to his Excellency and to the Parliament
If Venus Lady of the eighth, viz his second, is in Exaltation, then The Defendant a man of good estate, or ably to spend well
If Sun and Moon in Cancer, then The Judge would labour to compound the matter betwixt both parties
If Dispositor of the Sun in his Fall, viz Venus in Virgo with the Moon, then Great expense of the querents or Plaintiffs estate and money in this Suit
If Saturn and Mars are in Aries, then Such a man as Saturn would herein be a great enemy unto him
If Mars is Lord of the third, then He may show an ill Neighbour, or a Brother or Kinsman
If The third house is the ninth from the seventh, then It may argue some pragmaticall Priest, or one of the Defendants Sisters Husbands
If Mars is Saturnine, then Then shall he receive prejudice by his extreme rigid following the Cause
If Mars signifies his Lawyer, then The damage is by him, or by some aged man, perhaps the querents Father or Grandfather, or else some sturdy Clowne or ill Tenant
If Lord of his ascendant in the fourth or eighth, either from his owne ascendant, or of the Figure, then The man or woman enquired after, is deceased
If Lord of his ascendant or the Moon be in the twelfth From his owne house with any evill Planet, then Then the absent is dead
If Lord of his ascendant or the Moon be in the twelfth, in aspect of any unfortunate Planet either by conjunction or opposition, then Then the absent is dead
If The Sun be unfortunate or afflicted or the Moon in like manner, then Then the absent is dead

If Significator of the absent man or woman be in the sixth from his owne house or sixt of the Question or in any square or opposition or affliction of the Lord of the sixt without Reception, or the benevolent aspect of a Fortune, then The absent is then sick
If Significator of the absent man or woman be but going unto, and is not separated from the aspect he hath, then He has not been but he will be suddenly ill or very shortly

If Significator of the absent man or woman be going from the aspect of evill Planets either by body or aspect so that he be surely separated from them or is departing from Combustion, then It argues the party enquired of hath lately escaped a danger or sicknesiseor perill equivalent

If Significator of the absent party be in the sixt and not joyned to the Lord of the sixt or to any unfortunate Planet affixing him, then You must not then Judge the man sick but rather weary or drowfie or perhaps he hath let blood of late andc. or taken some Physick sor prevention of a disease which he seared

If Significator of the absent party be in any amicable aspect with either of the Fortunes, then You must not then Judge the man sick but rather weary or drowfie or perhaps he hath let blood of late andc. or taken some Physick sor prevention of a disease which he seared

If Significator of the absent party be strong in the Sign, then You must not then Judge the man sick but rather weary or drowfie or perhaps he hath let blood of late andc. or taken some Physick sor prevention of a disease which he seared
If Significator of the absent be strong and separated from a Fortunes and in a good house, then The absent lives
If Significator of the absent be afflicted, or Was lately in square or and of the Infortunes, then He was perplexed or suffered much misery according to the nature of the house from whence affiianded
If Lord of the eighth doe unfortunate him, then I judge him not dead
If His significator be in the first house, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne
If His significator be in the ninth, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne
If His significator be in the twelft, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne
If His significator be in the fift, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne
If His significator be in the third, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne
If He be in any of these houses, or do commit his disposition to any Planet in any of these houses, then The absent will not dye in that Voyage but returne

If His significator be in the seventh, then He will returne but not in hast ; nay he will tarry long ; and he is at time of the Ctiieftion in that country unto which he first wentnor hath he hitherto had any thoughts of returning ; howsoever now he hath
If Significator in the second house in aspect with a planet in the fifth house, then The absent is endeavoring to provide moneys for his voyage homewards and will be home soon.
If Significator in a cadent house and not beholding the ascendant, then He neither cares for his return, or has any thoughts thereof, nor can he come if he would.
If Significator cadent and afflicted and not beholding the ascendant but is otherwise impeded, then There’s no hopes of his return nor will he ever come.
If Significator Retrograde and beholding the ascendant, then Imports his sudden return when not expected.
If Moon joined to a Retrograde Planet and beholding the ascendant, then Imports his sudden return when not expected.
If Lord of the fourth house afflicts the significator, then The man is detained and cannot have liberty.
If Lord of the sixth house afflicts the significator, then He is ill.
If Lord of the eighth house afflicts the significator, then He fears he shall die by the way or before he gets into his own Country.
If Lord of the twelfth house afflicts the significator, then He is as a prisoner and cannot procure liberty.
If Moon in good aspect of the absent’s significator, then Argues his return.
If Moon commits her disposition unto the absent’s significator, and he posited in the ascendant, then Argues his return.
If Significator posited in the eighth, without other impediment, then Prolongs his return, but at last he will come.
If Significator unfortunated in the eighth, then He dies and never returns.

If Significator separating from the Lord of the fourth, seventh, ninth or third, or any Planet under the earth, and then joined to the Lord of the ascendant, or a Planet above the earth, then The absent will return.
If Both the Significators applying by trine or sextile aspect, then You shall hear some news of the party, or have a letter from him, or concerning him.
If Both the Signisicators come to conjunction, then He will come home and the Querent shall be in his company.

If Significator of the absent be in any Sign preceding one of his own houses, then Observe how many degrees he wants ere he gets out of that Sign and enters his own house, and put them into days, weeks, months or years, according to discretion, and the nature of the Sign and place of heaven he is in.
If Lord of the ascendant is joined to that Planet who afflicts him with a complete conjunction, then The Querent shall be secure until that time, and year or years dignified by that conjunction.

If Lord of the first is joined to the Lord of the eighth or to the above said Infortune, who receives him not but afflicts him and is the interficient Planet, then He is like to die when their perfect conjunction is whether by body or aspect.

If Lord of the eighth and the Lord of the first be joined together and each receive other or at leastwise, the Lord of the first receive the Lord of the eight, then It prenotes his death as aforesaid.
If The malevolent Interfester comes to the degree of the Zodiac wherein the Lord of the ascendant was at time of the Question, then He is like to die.
If The unfortunate Anareta transits the degree ascending and there meets with the malevolent aspect of the Lord of the sixth, then He is like to die.

If An Eclipse or its opposite place falls to be either the degree ascending or the degree of the Sign wherein the Lord of the ascendant was or of the Moon if you judged by her and not by the Lord of the ascendant, then He is like to die.

If Lord of the first or the Moon is joined by body or violent aspect to the Lord of the eighth or infortunate Planet in the eighth, then You shall determine the death of the Querent by her or distance of degrees which the Lord of the eighth or of that Planet to whom either the Lord of the ascendant or Moon is joined.
If The above said conjunction be in a succedant house, then It notes so many months.
If The Sign be fixed, then It gives half years, half months.
If The above said conjunction be in a cadent house, then It notes so many weeks.

If The Lord of the first be separated from the Lord of the eighth, or the Lord of the eighth from him, or from that Planet who did afflict him, then It's not then probable the Querent shall die, in so many years as there are degrees betwixt them viz. from that their separation.
If The Moon be well fortified and the Lord of the ascendant be weak and afflicted, then The strength of the Moon assists nothing for the evasion of the querent.
If Lord of the ascendant is weak and afflicted, then The strength of the ascendant avails nothing for the evasion of the querent.
If Ascendant or seventh house has any malevolent planet posited therein, then Shows much about who will die first.
If A malefic fixed star rises with the ascendant or descends with the cusp of the seventh, then Shows much about who will die first.
If Lord of the seventh or Lord of the ascendant goes to combustion, then Shows much about who will die first.
If Lord of the ascendant is most afflicted, or first goes to combustion, and the first house itself is unfortunated by the presence of a malefic, then The querent dies first.
If The same misfortunes befall to the seventh house and its Lord, then The quesited dies first.
If Lord of the eighth is posited in the eighth, or what Planet is nearest to the cusp of the house, and has Dignities therein, then Take signification of the quality of death from either of these.
If Planet afflicts the Lord of the ascendant, and have Dignities in the eighth, then Take signification of the quality of death from this planet.
If Venus or Jupiter signifies death, then The querent shall die a fair death.
If Good Planets in the eighth, then A fair, gentle death.
If Malevolent planets in the eighth, then Either strong fevers, or long continued sicknesses, and much afflicting.
If Cusp of the eighth in the terms of Venus or Jupiter, then Good hopes of wealth.
If Venus or Jupiter posited in the eighth house, then Plenty of wealth if they are essentially dignified, direct, and free from Combustion.
If Venus or Jupiter posited in the eighth house are Retrograde, Combust, or slow in motion, then Not so much wealth.
If Lord of the eighth in the eighth no-ways impedited, then Gives good hopes of some Inheritance or Land to fall to the wife or woman, or by some Legacy, some Estate.

If Lord of the fourth in the figure, or the Lord of the tenth and the Lord of the eight be in any benevolent aspect out of Angles or succedant houses, or out of the eleventh and eight, then More certain inheritance or legacy.
If Sun in the eighth and in Taurus or Sagittarius, or any of the houses of Venus or Jupiter, they casting their trine or sextile aspect to Sun, then The estate of the quesited party is sufficient.

If The dispositor of Sun casts his sextile or trine to it, or else is in a good aspect of Venus or Jupiter, then The Woman inquired after to be a good Fortune, and you are not to make doubt of his or her Estate.
If Saturn or Mars Peregrine in the eighth, then Either poor or little of what is promised will be obtained, or extreme contention about it.
If Lord of the eighth Combust, then Slow performance, scarce ability in the Parents to perform what is promised.
If Moon in the eighth, no fortunate Planet being there, then There’s cheating intended, or more will be promised than performed.
If Lord of the eighth in the second, or in trine or sextile to the Lord of the second, then The querent shall have what is promised.
If Lord of the eighth in square to the Lord of the second, then The querent shall have what is promised with difficulty.
If Lord of the eighth in opposition to the Lord of the second, then The querent shall never have what is promised, without much wrangling.
If Lord of the eighth in the second, or in aspect to the Lord of the second with no reception, then Hardly at all.

If Moon is infortunate, or if the Lord of the ascendant be infortunate, and falling from an angle, especially from the twelfth and Moon with him, then The same Fear is true, and certain that there is cause for it, or that great labor and grief shall molest him, and that many things shall be demanded of him, or he charged with many matters not appertaining to him, or of which he is guilty.

If Lord of the ascendant ascends from the twelfth into the eleventh or tenth, or shall be joined to Fortunes, then The thing feared shall not appertain to him, or he be molested thereby, or that he need not be afraid, nor shall the matter do him ill, but he shall escape that scare.
If Lord of the ascendant shall be in one degree with Fortunes, then No ill is towards the Querent.

If The Fortunes unto which the Lord of the ascendant does apply, or which apply unto him be in the mid heaven, and the Moon apply unto those Fortunes, and she be in an angle or elevated above him, then He that is afraid shall easily be delivered from scare; nor has he any grounds for it.
If The significator of the question applying to infortunes, then It is true.
If The significator of the question applying to a fortune, and not received of an infortune, then It is false.

If Moon is in the eighth, sixth or twelfth, and apply to any Planet in a Cadent house, then The Suspicion is not true, or the report will not hold long, but that it will be withered and vanish to nothing.
If Moon in trine to Sun discovers all suddenly, then The Suspicion is not true, or the report will not hold long, but that it will be withered and vanish to nothing.

If Saturn shows long lingering Diseases and the Lord of the Sixth house was Retrograde, then she would relapse out of one Disease into another, partly by her own obstinateness, and partly by the error of the Physician
If the Lord of her ascendant was also Lord of the eighth, then relapse out of one Disease into another partly by her own wilfulness
If the Physician was signified by Jupiter in square with Saturn in Equinoctial and Tropical Signs and in Angles, then small care of the miserable Gentlewoman
If Significator of the Man has no manner of affiction, viz. Venus she being in her Hayne, and free from the lead manner of misfortune, then the Woman would die first
If Venus and Moon came to an ill aspect, then the Woman should die
If Moon separates from Jupiter and is in conjunction with Venus, then the Woman should hear of him in two or three days
If Venus and Mars did hasten to a conjunction in Aries, Venus being therein very potent, then about that time she would have certain news of her Husband, if happily he came not then home

If Saturn is Lord of the Signe, and now itr Retrograde, and now posited in the twelft of the Figure, or sourth from his ascendant, then the heavens represent him in condition of mind, of a violent spirit, turbulent and envious, a man involved in troubles, imprisoned
If Venus is Retrograde, and with many fixed Starres of the nature of Mars and Jupiter, then an argument he was deep laden with misfortunes and vulgar Clamours at this present

If Moon separates from Saturn, and applies to the conjunction of the Sun near the cusp of the eighth house, then he should not be hanged, but suffer a more noble kind of death, and that within the space of six or seven weeks, or thereabouts
If Jupiter is here Significant of the querent, Retrograde and in the 12th house, then the querent had been in some deep
If Venus in his Exaltation and Mars in fixed Signe, then the Woman to be well conceited of her selfe confident yet vertuous and modest
If Moon her Signisicant neer the Sun, then she had a scar near her right eye
If Sun in the eighth house which is the womans second, then he had no cause to scare the not-payment of his Wives Portion but that it would really be performed whatsoever was or had been promised

If Moon did separate from the conjunction of Sun, and transferred his vertue to Jupiter who is Lord of the ascendant and Disposer of Sun and also Lord of the querent's Second house, then he had no cause to scare the not-payment of his Wives Portion but that it would really be performed whatsoever was or had been promised

If good Planets have either government, or position thereof or aspect and the same without the testimonies of the Infortunes, then it signifieth good, or is an argument thereof in all questions concerning this house
If the ninth house is good and strong, and aspected of good Planets, or good Planets in the said house, especially if the Lord of the ascendant and the tenth be well affected, then it is very good
If Saturn, Mars or the Dragon's Tail there, then the way is evill
If the Lord of the ninth be with an evill Planet, then it is evill, and he shall not speed well in the way, or get any wealth by that Voyage
If Mars in the ninth, then danger by Theeves or Pyrates
If Saturn threatneth, then loss of Goods, or sickness
If the Dragon's Head doth import the same that Mars doth, then cozenage and deceit
If Fortunes there, then expect Wealth
If Infortunes there, then Ioffe
If the ninth infortunate, then many hardships in the Voyage
If Fortunes there, then a happy passage
If the Lord of the eighth,or eighth house be strong, then there is Wealth to be got
If Saturn, Mars or Dragon's Tail in that house, then nothing to be had, or not worth labour
If the Lord of the ascendant be with a good Planet or Planets, and they strong, and in a friendly aspect, then it signifieth good winds
If the Lord of the ascendant be with evill Planets, or they in cadent houses, then bad winds
If Lord of the eighth,or eighth house strong, then Wealth to be got
If Taurus, Cancer, or Leo in the eighth house, then nothing to be had, or not worth labor
If Lord of the ascendant with a good Planet or Planets, and they strong, and in a friendly aspect, then good winds
If Lord of the ascendant with evil Planets, or they in cadent houses, then contrary winds

If Significators in cadent houses out of fixed Signs, and near violent fixed stars, then Traveller may expect impetuous storms, sudden blasts, contrary winds often driving the Passenger this way, and sometimes that way , as also, that he shall await many dayes, weeks or moneths for a comfortable wind before he shall gaine it
If Significators in trine aspect out of airy Signs, viz Gemini, Libra or Aquarius, then Gentle gusts of wind
If Good Fortune in the ascendant, then good success, before that he removeth from the place he is in, or in the beginning of his Journey
If Good Fortune in the Midheaven, then much happiness in his Journey, and after his entring the Ship, or upon the way as he goeth on his Journey
If Fortune in the seventh, then content in the place whereunto he goeth
If Planet which is the Fortune in the fourth, then it shall be at his retorne, or when lie shall come to his owne place or home back againe

If Fortune in the tenth house is Venus, then he shall have good, or make great benesit in his Journey, by dealing in such things as bring joy, or cause delight and pastime, by Jewels or Silks which are of the nature of Venus
If Infortune be Lord of the twelfth and he be Saturn, then it is to be feared there shall happen unto him some sicknefte comming of a cold and dry cause, or by much trechery
If Saturn be in a Signe Bestiall, then it is to be feared, some ill sortune or mifchance shall happen to the Traveller by meanes of some Four-sooted Bead
If Saturn be in a humane Sign, then it may be by some deceitfull ill men

If ill Fortune instead of Saturn be Mars and Sun, then it is to be feared he may incurre some sickneffe that is of an hot nature, or fall into the hands of Theeves,and shall have seare of himself touching hurt to his body

If Significr of the Journey is swift in course, Occidental ,and and he in movable Signs, then his journy to be short,and not much tarrying from home, or a quick, speedy passage, yet with trouble and pain
If Significator is swift in motion and Oriental, then quick, short, and without any labour or much trouble
If Lord of the ascendant doth apply unto the Lord of the house of Journeys, then short return or good speed according to the length of the Voyager
If Lord of the Journey applies to the Lord of the ascendant, then short return or good speed according to the length of the Voyager
If any Planet carryeth light of one to the other, then short return or good speed according to the length of the Voyager
